Example Sentence + [Translation]: Բարև, ինչպե՞ս ես։ - Շնորհակալություն, ամեն ինչ լավ է։ [Hello, how are you? - Thanks, all is well.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. Ամեն (amen) means 'every'. Ինչ (inch') means 'thing'. Լավ (lav) means 'good'. Է (e) is the verb 'to be' (is). Literally: "Every thing good is."
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): "Amen, inch is all well," the preacher said, pointing at the inchworm on the good bread.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, ասա՛ ճշմարտությունը։ [Please, say the truth.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: ասել (asel). Imperative (singular, informal): ասա՛ (asa).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I *assure* you, just *say* it!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Արի՛ այստեղ, մի բան ցույց տամ։ [Come here, let me show you something.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: գալ (gal). Imperative (singular, informal): արի՛ (ari). Note the irregular imperative form.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): An *ari*a from the opera makes everyone *come* to the window.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ափսոս, որ չկարողացար գալ։ [It's a pity that you couldn't come.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Interjection/Adverb. Root: ափսոս (ap'sos). No changes for case or number.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): *Oops, so sad* it's a pity I dropped my phone.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ընթրիքը պատրաստ է, բոլորիդ բարի ախորժակ։ [Dinner is ready, enjoy your meal everyone.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. Բարի (Bari) means 'good'. Ախորժակ (akhorzhak) means 'appetite'. Literally: "Good appetite."
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): *Barry* the *acrobat* always wished us a "good appetite" before he ate.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ներողություն, ժամը քանի՞սն է։ [Excuse me, what time is it?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. Ժամը (Zham@) is 'the hour'. Քանիսն (k'anisn) is 'which number'. Է (e) is 'is'. Literally: "The hour is which number?"
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): "What time is it?" The genie in the *jam* jar asked, "*How nice* it is to be free!"

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս գիրքը ի՞նչ արժե։ [How much does this book cost?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. Ինչ (Inch') means 'what'. Արժե (arzhe) means 'it costs' or 'it is worth'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): *How much* does an *inch* of this *azure* blue fabric cost?

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ողջույն, Արամ, ի՞նչ կա չկա։ [Hi, Aram, what's up?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Colloquial Phrase. Literally "What is there, what isn't there?". Կա (ka) means 'there is'. Չկա (ch'ka) means 'there is not'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): *Inch ka ch'ka*? I have an *itch* to *catch a* movie, what's up with you?

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ինձ պետք է մի բաժակ ջուր։ [I need a glass of water.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. Ինձ (Indz) is the dative case of 'I' (to me). Պետք է (p'etk' e) means 'is necessary'. The construction is impersonal: "To me is necessary..."
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): *I need* a *pet cat*, so I'll go to the shelter *in this* taxi.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Կե՛ր այս համեղ խնձորը։ [Eat this delicious apple.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: ուտել (utel). Imperative (singular, informal): կե՛ր (ker). Note the irregular imperative form.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I *care* that you *eat* your vegetables!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս ժամացույցը շատ գեղեցիկ է, բայց շատ թանկ է։ [This watch is very beautiful, but it's very expensive.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. Շատ (Shat) means 'very' or 'a lot'. Թանկ (t'ank) means 'expensive'. Է (e) is 'is'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I *shot* the *tank* with a water pistol, but it was *very expensive* to replace.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Բարև ձեզ, ես Աննան եմ։ - Շատ հաճելի է։ [Hello, I am Anna. - Nice to meet you.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. Շատ (Shat) means 'very'. Հաճելի (hacheli) means 'pleasant'. Է (e) is 'is'. Literally: "It is very pleasant."
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): *It's nice to meet you*, said the jolly man, "*Shaaat*, that's a *hot chili*!"

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Սո՛ւս, երեխան քնած է։ [Hush, the baby is sleeping.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Interjection. Root: սուս (sus). Onomatopoeic.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The spy said "*Hush*!" because he was *sus*picious.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Տա՛ր այս գրքերը գրադարան։ [Take these books to the library.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: տանել (tanel). Imperative (singular, informal): տա՛ր (tar).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): *Take* the sticky *tar* off the road.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ինձ մի քիչ ջուր տո՛ւր։ [Give me a little water.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: տալ (tal). Imperative (singular, informal): տո՛ւր (tur).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): *Give* me a *tour* of the city!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես այսօր երեկոյան ազատ եմ։ [I am free this evening.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: ազատ (azat). Does not change for case or number when used as a predicate.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He was *free* to go after he *ate a lot*.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ ազգանունը Պետրոսյան է։ [My surname is Petrosyan.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: ազգանուն (azganun). Compound word: ազգ (azg - 'nation/clan') + անուն (anun - 'name'). Definite form: ազգանունը (azganun@).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My *surname* is "Ask a nun."

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս աթոռը շատ հարմարավետ է։ [This chair is very comfortable.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: աթոռ (at'orr). Definite form: աթոռը (at'orr@). Plural: աթոռներ (at'orrner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The author sat in his *chair*.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Գիշերը մենք գնալու ենք ակումբ։ [Tonight we are going to a club.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: ակումբ (akumb). Cognate with English "club". Definite form: ակումբը (akumb@). Plural: ակումբներ (akumbner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'll *accompany* you to the *club*.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նրա աղջիկը սովորում է համալսարանում։ [His daughter is studying at the university.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: աղջիկ (aghjik). Definite form: աղջիկը (aghjik@). Plural: աղջիկներ (aghjikner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A *girl* who is *all chic* and stylish.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ամռանը մենք միշտ գնում ենք լողալու։ [In the summer, we always go swimming.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: ամառ (amarr). Definite form: ամառը (amarr'y). The 'ռռ' (rr) sound is a trilled R, common in Armenian.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The only ARMOR I wear in the summer is sunscreen.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ լավագույն ընկերը ամերիկացի է։ [My best friend is American.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: Ամերիկա (Amerika). The suffix '-ացի' (-ats'i) indicates origin or nationality, like '-an' or '-er' in English. Plural: ամերիկացիներ (amerikats'iner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): An AMERICAN SEE-s the world differently.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մեկ ամսից իմ ծնունդն է։ [My birthday is in one month.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: ամիս (amis). Definite form: ամիսը (amisy). Plural: ամիսներ (amisner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I AM MISS-ing you this past month.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նրա ամուսինը շատ բարի մարդ է։ [Her husband is a very kind person.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: ամուսին (amusin). Definite form: ամուսինը (amusiny). Related to ամուսնություն (amusnut'yun) - marriage.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My husband is AMUSING.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նրանք ուզում են ամուսնանալ հաջորդ տարի։ [They want to get married next year.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: ամուսնանալ (amusnanal). Imperative (informal, singular): ամուսնացիր (amusnats'ir). Root is related to 'amusin' (husband).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): To get married, you need A MOOSE AND AN AWL.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես ամուսնացած եմ և երկու երեխա ունեմ։ [I am married and have two children.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective/Participle. This is the past participle of the verb ամուսնանալ (amusnanal). It doesn't change form based on gender.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Since I'm married, I'm A MOOSE NOT SAD.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այդ գիրքը շատ հետաքրքիր է։ [That book is very interesting.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Demonstrative Adjective. Used for something near the listener. Part of the series: այս (ays - this), այդ (ayd - that, near you), այն (ayn - that, over there).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'D like THAT one, please.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ի՞նչ ես անում այդտեղ։ [What are you doing there?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb of place. Combination of այդ (ayd - that) + տեղ (t'egh - place). Refers to a place near the listener.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'D TAKE you THERE, since it's close to you.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այն սարի հետևում մի գյուղ կա։ [There is a village behind that mountain (over there).]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Demonstrative Adjective. Used for something far from both the speaker and the listener. Corresponds to the place adverb այնտեղ (aynt'egh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): EIN-stein pointed to THAT distant star.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ տունը այնտեղ է, գետի մյուս կողմում։ [My house is over there, on the other side of the river.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb of place. Combination of այն (ayn - that over there) + տեղ (t'egh - place). Refers to a place far from both speaker and listener.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I AIN'T going OVER THERE.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: - Դու սուրճ սիրու՞մ ես։ - Այո, շատ։ [- Do you like coffee? - Yes, very much.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb. 'Այո' (ayo) is the formal/standard 'yes'. 'Հա' (ha) is the informal, everyday 'yes', similar to 'yeah'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): AYO: 'I OWE' you a yes. HA: 'Ha ha ha', yes, that's funny.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս խնձորը շատ համեղ է։ [This apple is very tasty.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Demonstrative Adjective. Used for something near the speaker. Corresponds to the place adverb այստեղ (ayst'egh - here).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): THIS is as cold as ICE.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Եկեք նստենք այստեղ՝ ծառի տակ։ [Let's sit here, under the tree.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb of place. Combination of այս (ays - this) + տեղ (t'egh - place). Refers to the speaker's location.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): ICE STAYS HERE in the freezer.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այսօր եղանակը հրաշալի է։ [The weather is wonderful today.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb of time. Compound word: այս (ays - this) + օր (or - day). Literally 'this day'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'M SORE TODAY after my workout.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ջուրը անգույն հեղուկ է։ [Water is a colorless liquid.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Prefix 'ան-' (an-) means 'without' or '-less'. The root is 'գույն' (guyn), which means 'color'. Literally 'without-color'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): An ANGRY TWIN is COLORLESS with rage.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես պետք է տնային աշխատանքս անեմ։ [I have to do my homework.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: անել (anel). Imperative (informal, singular): արա (ara). One of the most common and irregular verbs.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): ANN'LL DO it later.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա անընդհատ խոսում է հեռախոսով։ [She constantly talks on the phone.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb. Formed from 'ան-' (an- 'without') and 'ընդհատել' (yndhatel 'to interrupt'). So, 'without interruption'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): ON AND HOT, the sun shone CONSTANTLY.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մի՛ եղիր այդքան անհանգիստ, ամեն ինչ լավ կլինի։ [Don't be so worried, everything will be fine.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Prefix 'ան-' (an- 'without') + 'հանգիստ' (hangist 'calm/rest'). Literally 'without rest' or 'without calm'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): AN HONEST person is never WORRIED about lying.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ինչպե՞ս է քո անունը։ [What is your name?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: անուն (anun). Definite form: անունը (anuny). Cognate with English 'name' through Proto-Indo-European.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A NUN needs a holy NAME.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ աշխատավայրը գտնվում է քաղաքի կենտրոնում։ [My workplace is located in the city center.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Compound word: աշխատել (ashkhatel - to work) + վայր (vayr - place). Literally 'work-place'. Definite form: աշխատավայրը (ashkhatavayry).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'd ASK OTTO for a buyer at my WORKPLACE.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես պետք է շատ աշխատեմ այս շաբաթ։ [I have to work a lot this week.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: աշխատել (ashkhatel). Imperative: աշխատի՛ր (ashkhati'r). The root is related to 'աշխատանք' (ashkhatank') meaning 'work'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I have to 'ash my cat's tail' because I work so hard.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Աշունը իմ սիրելի եղանակն է, որովհետև տերևները գունավորվում են։ [Autumn is my favorite season because the leaves change color.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: աշուն (ashun). Definite form: աշունը (ashunə).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Ah, soon' it will be autumn and the leaves will fall.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մայրիկիս պատրաստած ոսպով ապուրը շատ համեղ է։ [My mom's lentil soup is very delicious.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: ապուր (apur). Definite form: ապուրը (apurə).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I want 'a purr'-fectly warm bowl of soup.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ տատիկն ու պապիկն ապրում են գյուղում։ [My grandparents live in the village.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: ապրել (aprel). Imperative: ապրի՛ր (apri'r). Also used in the cheer 'ապրե՛ս' (apres!) meaning 'well done!' or 'live long!'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I 'applaud' everyone who is able to live a happy life.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ապրիլին ծառերը սկսում են ծաղկել։ [In April, the trees start to blossom.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: ապրիլ (april). A cognate with English 'April', making it easy to remember.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): This is a freebie, it sounds exactly like 'April'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Առավոտյան սուրճ եմ խմում, որ արթնանամ։ [I drink coffee in the morning to wake up.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: առավոտ (arravot). Definite form: առավոտը (arravotə).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): An 'arrow' 'shot' at my alarm clock in the morning.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երբ կավարտես դասերդ, կարող ենք գնալ զբոսնելու։ [When you finish your homework, we can go for a walk.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: ավարտել (avartel). Imperative: ավարտի՛ր (avarti'r). The root 'ավարտ' (avart) means 'end' or 'finish'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Ah, Vart, tell' me when you finish your story.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հաջորդ ավտոբուսը կգա տասը րոպեից։ [The next bus will come in ten minutes.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: ավտոբուս (avtobus). An international word, cognate with 'autobus' or 'bus'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): An 'auto bus' is a bus that drives itself.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես վախենում եմ ատամնաբույժի գնալուց։ [I am afraid of going to the dentist.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Compound word: ատամ (atam) 'tooth' + բույժ (buyzh) 'healer'. Definite form: ատամնաբույժը (atamnabuyzhə).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): At a man, a booze' bottle flew and hit his teeth, so he went to the dentist.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս մեքենան շատ արագ է գնում։ [This car goes very fast.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: արագ (arag).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): That car is as fast as 'a rag' blowing in the wind.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սովորաբար արթնանում եմ առավոտյան ժամը յոթին։ [I usually wake up at seven in the morning.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: արթնանալ (art'nanal). Imperative: արթնացի՛ր (art'nats'ir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Art's none all' over the bed when I wake up.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երևանում շատ արտասահմանցի ուսանողներ կան։ [There are many foreign students in Yerevan.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Compound word: արտ (art) 'outside' + սահման (sahman) 'border' + -ցի (-ts'i) suffix for origin.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The foreigner points and says 'Art is a man, see?'

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այսօր արևը շատ պայծառ է շողում։ [The sun is shining very brightly today.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: արև (arev). Definite form: արևը (arevə).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Let's 'have a rave' under the sun!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Պապիկս սիրում է նստել բազկաթոռին և թերթ կարդալ։ [My grandpa likes to sit in the armchair and read the newspaper.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Compound word: բազուկ (bazuk) 'arm' + աթոռ (at'orr) 'chair'. Definite form: բազկաթոռը (bazkat'orrə).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A 'bazooka tore' a giant hole in my favorite armchair.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ընտանիքով հավաքվել ենք բազմոցին և ֆիլմ ենք դիտում։ [The family has gathered on the sofa and is watching a movie.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. From the root 'բազում' (bazum) meaning 'many', as a sofa is for many people. Definite form: բազմոցը (bazmots'ə).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Pass mots'arella sticks, we're watching the game on the sofa.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, ինձ մի բաժակ ջուր տվեք։ [Please give me a glass of water.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: բաժակ (bazhak). Definite form: բաժակը (bazhakə).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'll have 'a Jack' Daniels in a glass.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նրանք երկար տարիներ ամուսնացած էին, բայց վերջերս բաժանվեցին։ [They were married for many years, but recently they got divorced.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. From 'բաժանել' (bazhanel) 'to separate'. The -վել (-vel) ending indicates a reflexive or passive action.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): They got divorced because he wore 'pajama velvet' every single night.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հայրիկը բահով այգին էր փորում։ [Dad was digging the garden with a shovel.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: բահ (bah). Definite form: բահը (bahə).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Bah!' Humbug! I hate digging with this shovel.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Բաղնիքը երկրորդ հարկում է։ [The bathroom is on the second floor.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: բաղնիք (baghnik'). Definite form: բաղնիքը (baghnik'ə).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My 'bag' of 'knick-knacks' fell into the toilet in the bathroom.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես ուզում էի գնալ կինո, բայց տոմս չկար։ [I wanted to go to the cinema, but there were no tickets.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Conjunction. Root: բայց (bayts'). Connects contrasting ideas.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I wanted to go, 'but' the guard dog 'bites'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես նախաճաշին բանան եմ ուտում։ [I eat a banana for breakfast.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: բանան (banan). Definite form: բանանը (banan'y). An international word, identical in sound and meaning to its English counterpart.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Sounds exactly like 'banana'. Imagine a banana wearing a bandanna to make it memorable.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես պետք է գնամ բանկ՝ փող հանելու։ [I need to go to the bank to withdraw money.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: բանկ (bank). Definite form: բանկը (bank'y). This is a direct cognate with the English word 'bank'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Sounds just like 'bank'. Picture a huge piggy bank that is also a building for a bank.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Շուկայում թարմ բանջարեղեն են վաճառում։ [They sell fresh vegetables at the market.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun (collective). Root: բանջար (banjar). Definite form: բանջարեղենը (banjareghen'y). 'Բանջար' means 'greens/herbs', and '-եղեն' is a suffix for 'things made of'. So, 'vegetable-things'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Sounds like 'ban jar again'. You must 'ban' the rabbit from the vegetable 'jar again' and again.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ընկերներով գնացինք բար մի գավաթ գարեջուր խմելու։ [We went to the bar with friends to drink a glass of beer.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: բար (bar). Definite form: բարը (bar'y). An international word.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It's the same as 'bar'. Imagine a big brown bear sitting at the bar sipping a drink.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա շատ բարակ գիրք էր կարդում։ [She was reading a very thin book.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: բարակ (barak). It is invariable and does not change form.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): barak' sounds like 'Barack'. Imagine former president Barack Obama being so thin he can slide under a door.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա շատ բարի մարդ է, միշտ օգնում է բոլորին։ [He is a very kind person, he always helps everyone.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: բարի (bari). This word is the root for all 'good' greetings like 'good morning' (bari luys).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): bari' sounds like 'bury'. A kind (bari) friend helps you 'bury' your troubles with kindness.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Բարի գալուստ Հայաստան։ [Welcome to Armenia!]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. A combination of բարի (bari - good) and գալուստ (galust - coming/arrival). Literally means 'good coming'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): bari galust' sounds like 'Good, a gallon of dust!' Imagine arriving somewhere dusty and exclaiming this. Welcome!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Բարի գիշեր, քաղցր երազներ։ [Good night, sweet dreams.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. Composed of բարի (bari - good) + գիշեր (gisher - night).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): For a 'bari gisher' (good night), you hope nobody 'gears' up their loud motorcycle or 'shares' loud music.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Բարի երեկո, ինչպե՞ս եք։ [Good evening, how are you?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. Composed of բարի (bari - good) + երեկո (ereko - evening).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): bari ereko' sounds like 'good echo'. A good evening is so quiet you can hear an echo.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Բարի լույս, սիրելիս։ [Good morning, my love.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. Literally 'good light'. Composed of բարի (bari - good) + լույս (luys - light).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): bari luys' sounds like 'good loose'. Hope you have a good morning and don't get 'loose' with your schedule.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Վաղը մեկնում եմ, չէ՞։ Բարի ճանապարհ։ [You're leaving tomorrow, right? Have a nice journey.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. Literally 'good road'. Composed of բարի (bari - good) + ճանապարհ (chanaparh - road/journey).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): For a 'bari chanaparh' (good journey), you 'channel a path' on the map.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Բարի օր, կարո՞ղ եմ օգնել ձեզ։ [Good day, can I help you?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. Literally 'good day'. Composed of բարի (bari - good) + օր (or - day).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): bari or' sounds like 'good, or...' It's a funny, threatening way to wish someone a good day: 'Have a good day... OR ELSE!'

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ինչու՞ ես այդքան բարկացած, ի՞նչ է պատահել։ [Why are you so angry, what happened?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Participle (acting as an adjective). From the verb բարկանալ (barkanal - to get angry).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): barkats'ats' sounds like a dog that 'barks at cats'. It's very angry!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Բարև, Արամ, ո՞նց ես։ [Hello, Aram, how are you?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Greeting (informal). Root: բարև (barev). A very common, everyday greeting.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): barev' sounds like 'bear-ev'. Imagine saying hello to a friendly bear you meet every evening.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Բարև ձեզ, տիկին Պողոսյան։ [Hello, Mrs. Poghosyan.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Greeting (formal/plural). Composed of բարև (barev - hello) + ձեզ (dzez - to you). Used for elders, strangers, or groups.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): You say 'barev' to a bear, but you say 'barev dzez' when the bear has 'desserts' and you need to be polite.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խանութը բաց է մինչև ժամը ինը։ [The store is open until nine o'clock.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: բաց (bats'). Related to the verb բացել (bats'el - to open).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The door is open, so watch out for the 'bats' flying in.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Կարո՞ղ ես, խնդրում եմ, պատուհանը բացել։ [Can you please open the window?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: բացել (bats'el). Imperative (singular, informal): բա՛ց (bats').
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): To open the door, you need to get rid of the 'bats'. You yell, 'Bats, sell! I'm selling bats!'

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, մի բաժակ ջուր բերել։ [Please bring a glass of water.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: բերել (berel). Imperative (singular, informal): բե՛ր (ber).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): berel' sounds like 'bear ale'. Imagine a large bear bringing you a mug of ale.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Եթե վատ ես զգում, պետք է բժշկի գնաս։ [If you feel unwell, you should go to the doctor.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: բժիշկ (bzhishk). Definite form: բժիշկը (bzhishk'y). From the verb բուժել (buzhel - to heal).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): bzhishk' sounds like a buzzing bee sting 'bzzz-shk'. The doctor gives you a shot that feels like a bee sting.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա հաջողակ բիզնես ունի Երևանում։ [He has a successful business in Yerevan.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: բիզնես (biznes). Definite form: բիզնեսը (biznes'y). An international loanword.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Sounds exactly like 'business'. Imagine a bee that is very busy with its business.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նրա հայրը հաջողակ բիզնեսմեն է։ [His father is a successful businessman.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: բիզնես (biznes). A loanword from English/Russian. The definite form is բիզնեսմենը (biznesmenuh). Plural: բիզնեսմեններ (biznesmenner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Sounds just like 'businessman'. No cheat needed, he's a 'business man'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մենք բարձրացանք բլուրը՝ քաղաքը տեսնելու համար։ [We climbed the hill to see the city.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: բլուր (blur). Definite form: բլուրը (blurruh). Plural: բլուրներ (blurrner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The view from the hill was a 'blur' because of the fog.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նրանք նոր բնակարան են գնել Երևանի կենտրոնում։ [They bought a new apartment in the center of Yerevan.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: բնակ- (bnak-, 'to dwell/inhabit'). Derived from բնակվել (bnakvel - to live). Definite form: բնակարանը (bnakaranuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My 'banana car ran' right into my new apartment building.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Բուժքույրը հոգատարությամբ խնամում էր հիվանդին։ [The nurse was carefully taking care of the patient.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound word: բուժ- (buzh-, 'to heal') + քույր (k'uyr, 'sister'). Literally 'healing sister'. Definite form: բուժքույրը (buzhk'uyruh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The nurse said, 'Push, queer patient!' which was strange.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Արարատի գագաթը ձյունով է պատված։ [The peak of Ararat is covered with snow.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: գագաթ (gagat'). Definite form: գագաթը (gagat'uh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I went 'gaga' at the top/peak of the mountain.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Դու վաղը մեզ հյուր կգա՞ս։ [Will you come visit us tomorrow?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: գալ (gal). Imperative (singular): արի (ari). This is an irregular verb.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The 'gal' will 'come' to the party later.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Գարունը տարվա իմ ամենասիրելի եղանակն է։ [Spring is my favorite season of the year.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: գարուն (garun). Definite form: գարունը (garunuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Let's 'go run' in the park now that it's spring.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս ապուրի համար ինձ մեծ գդալ է պետք։ [I need a big spoon for this soup.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: գդալ (gdal). Definite form: գդալը (gdaluh). Plural: գդալներ (gdalner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It's a 'good doll', now eat your soup with the spoon.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հայաստանում շատ գեղեցիկ լեռներ կան։ [There are many beautiful mountains in Armenia.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: գեղ- (gegh- 'beauty'). Does not change for gender or number.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'll 'get sick' if I look at something so beautiful for too long.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Արաքս գետը հոսում է Հայաստանի սահմանով։ [The Araks river flows along the border of Armenia.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: գետ (get). Definite form: գետը (getuh). Plural: գետեր (geter).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I have to 'get' across the river.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Գիշերը երկինքը լի էր աստղերով։ [At night, the sky was full of stars.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: գիշեր (gisher). Definite form: գիշերը (gisheruh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The candy will 'get shared' among the kids at night.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես գիտեմ, որ դու ճիշտ ես։ [I know that you are right.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: գիտենալ (gitenal). Imperative (singular): իմացի՛ր (imats'ir). Conjugates as գիտեմ, գիտես, գիտի (gitem, gites, giti).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Get an al'-manac so you will 'know' the future.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս գիրքը շատ հետաքրքիր սյուժե ունի։ [This book has a very interesting plot.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: գիր (gir 'letter/writing'). Definite form: գիրքը (girk'uh). Plural: գրքեր (grk'er).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A 'geek' loves to read a book.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես պետք է հիմա խանութ գնամ։ [I have to go to the store now.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: գնալ (gnal). Imperative (singular): գնա՛ (gna).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It's 'gonna' get late, let's go now.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես շատ գոհ եմ իմ նոր աշխատանքից։ [I am very pleased with my new job.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. From Old Armenian. Does not change for gender or number.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Go' home now, I'm pleased with the work you've done.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա հայտնի գործարար է և բարերար։ [He is a famous businessman and philanthropist.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: գործ (gorts 'work/deed'). Definite form: գործարարը (gortsararuh). Plural: գործարարներ (gortsararrner). This is a native Armenian word.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): That businessman's 'car is rare' and expensive.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հայրս միշտ իր գործիքները կարգին է պահում։ [My father always keeps his tools in order.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: գործ (gorts 'work/deed'). The -իք (-ik') is a suffix for instruments. Definite form: գործիքը (gortsik'uh). Plural: գործիքներ (gortsik'ner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My project will 'go sick' if I don't use the right tool.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, երկու հարյուր գրամ պանիր կշռեք։ [Please, weigh two hundred grams of cheese.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Loanword. Definite form: գրամը (gramuh). Plural: գրամներ (gramner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Sounds exactly like the English word 'gram'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սիրում եմ նամակներ գրել իմ ընկերներին։ [I like to write letters to my friends.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: գրել (grel). Imperative (singular): գրի՛ր (grir). Root: գր- (gr-).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): After you 'grill' the meat, write down the recipe.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Կարո՞ղ եմ քո գրիչը վերցնել մի րոպեով։ [Can I take your pen for a minute?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: գր- (gr- 'to write'). The -իչ (-ich) is a suffix for instruments/agents. Definite form: գրիչը (grich'uh). Plural: գրիչներ (grich'ner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I 'reach' for my pen.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Դա իմ գիրքը չէ։ [That is not my book.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Demonstrative pronoun. Points to something relatively far from the speaker.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): "DA!" says the strict librarian, pointing at "that" overdue book.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, ինձ մի դանակ տուր։ [Please, give me a knife.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: դանակ (danak). Definite form: դանակը (danak@). Plural: դանակներ (danakner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He has 'DA KNACK' for chopping vegetables with a knife.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս դեղը շատ դառն է։ [This medicine is very bitter.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: դառն (darrn). The base form is դառը.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): This coffee is so bitter, 'DARR-N' it!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս շենքը կառուցվել է անցյալ դարում։ [This building was built in the last century.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: դար (dar). Definite form: դարը (dar@). Plural: դարեր (darer).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It took them a century to build the 'DARk' castle.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Դեկտեմբերին մենք զարդարում ենք տոնածառը։ [In December, we decorate the Christmas tree.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: դեկտեմբեր (dektember). Cognate with English 'December'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): In 'DEKTEMBER,' we 'deck the halls' for the holidays.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Գլխացավի դեղ գնելու համար պետք է գնալ դեղատուն։ [To buy headache medicine, you need to go to the pharmacy.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound Noun. From դեղ (degh - medicine) + տուն (tun - house). Literally 'medicine house'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The only radio station I get is 'DEGH-A-TUNE' from the pharmacy next door.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Արևը դեղին է և շատ պայծառ։ [The sun is yellow and very bright.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: դեղին (deghin).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The little yellow chick is 'DEGHIN' a hole in the ground. (digging)

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ֆրանսիայի դեսպանը ելույթ ունեցավ համալսարանում։ [The ambassador of France gave a speech at the university.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: դեսպան (despan). Definite form: դեսպանը (despan@).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Don't use 'THIS PAN' to cook for the ambassador.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Վիզա ստանալու համար պետք է գնալ դեսպանատուն։ [To get a visa, you must go to the embassy.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound Noun. From դեսպան (despan - ambassador) + տուն (tun - house). Literally 'ambassador house'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The embassy plays a special 'DESPAN-A-TUNE' every morning.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս դերասանը խաղում է իմ սիրելի ֆիլմում։ [This actor plays in my favorite movie.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: դերասան (derasan). Female form is դերասանուհի (derasanuhi).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): They 'DARE A SON' of theirs to become an actor.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ուսուցիչը դժգոհ էր իմ աշխատանքից։ [The teacher was displeased with my work.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Formed with the negative prefix դժ- (dzh-) + գոհ (goh - pleased).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The displeased customer shouted, 'DZH-GOH away!'

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Դիվանագետը պետք է լինի շատ զգույշ իր խոսքերում։ [A diplomat must be very careful with his words.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: դիվանագետ (divanaget). Definite form: դիվանագետը (divanaget@).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The diplomat wanted to 'DIVE-ON-A-JET' to get to the meeting.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սիրում եմ երեկոյան նորություններ դիտել։ [I like to watch the news in the evening.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: դիտել (ditel). Imperative: դիտի՛ր (ditir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): DI-TELL' me what you want to watch on TV.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Դու հայերեն հասկանու՞մ ես։ [Do you understand Armenian?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Pronoun. Second person singular, informal.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): DO' you know who I am talking to? I'm talking to you!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Թխկացրու դուռը մտնելուց առաջ։ [Knock on the door before entering.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: դուռ (durr). Definite form: դուռը (durr@).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A cat with a loud purr is waiting at the 'DOOR'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նրա դուստրը շատ գեղեցիկ է։ [His daughter is very beautiful.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: դուստր (dustr). Definite form: դուստրը (dustr@). Plural: դուստրեր (dustrer).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My daughter cleaned up all the 'DUST' for her mother.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երեխաները խաղում են դրսում։ [The children are playing outside.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb. Root: դուրս (durs).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He 'DURST' (burst) out the doors to get outside.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Դուք որտեղի՞ց եք։ [Where are you (formal/plural) from?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Pronoun. Second person plural or formal singular.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A 'DUCK' is asking 'you all' for some bread.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ամեն առավոտ ես գնում եմ դպրոց։ [Every morning I go to school.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: դպրոց (dprots'). Definite form: դպրոցը (dprots'@).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): DA PROS' all go to this school to get better.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս գլխարկը հինգ հազար դրամ արժե։ [This hat costs five thousand dram.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. The national currency of Armenia. Also means 'money' in general.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I had a 'DRAM' (dream) that I had a lot of money.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Դրանք իմ գրքերն են։ [Those are my books.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Demonstrative pronoun, plural of 'դա' (da - that).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Those guys drank all the juice!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ե՞րբ ես գալու։ [When are you coming?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Interrogative adverb. Often used at the beginning of a question.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): When did you plant that herb?

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես շատ եմ սիրում ելակ։ [I love strawberries very much.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: ելակը (elakuh). Plural: ելակներ (elakner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): There's a lack of strawberries in my dessert.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ եղբայրը բժիշկ է։ [My brother is a doctor.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: եղբայրը (eghbayruh). Root: 'եղբայր'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My brother is a spy who goes by Eghbayr.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես Հայաստանից եմ։ [I am from Armenia.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Personal pronoun, first person singular. Nominative case.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I am the best!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա տաղանդավոր երաժիշտ է։ [He/She is a talented musician.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: երաժիշտը (erazhishtuh). Derived from երաժշտություն (erazhshtut'yun - music).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The musician erased his bad notes.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երբեմն ես թեյ եմ խմում առավոտյան։ [Sometimes I drink tea in the morning.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb of frequency. Combines 'երբ' (erb - when) and a particle.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Herb men sometimes come to my garden.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես երբեք այնտեղ չեմ եղել։ [I have never been there.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb of frequency. Used with a negative verb (e.g., չեմ, չի).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I will never wear a fur beck-lace.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս երգիչը հիանալի ձայն ունի։ [This singer has a wonderful voice.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: երգիչը (ergich'uh). From the verb 'երգել' (ergel - to sing).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The singer got an ear itch from the loud music.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այգում շատ երեխաներ կան։ [There are many children in the park.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: երեխան (erekhan). Plural: երեխաներ (erekhaner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The child caused a wreck, ha!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երեկ ես կինո գնացի։ [Yesterday I went to the cinema.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb of time.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Yesterday, I had a wreck in my car.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս երեկո համերգ կա։ [There is a concert this evening.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: երեկոն (erekon). Related to 'երեկ' (erek - yesterday).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Eric, oh, it's already evening!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա երեսուն տարեկան է։ [He/She is 30 years old.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Cardinal number. Formed from 'երեք' (erek' - three) and 'սուն' (sun - ten, an old suffix).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Erase soon; we need 30 copies.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես երեք խնձոր ունեմ։ [I have three apples.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Cardinal number.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I have a wreck with 3 cars.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երեքշաբթի հանդիպում ունենք։ [We have a meeting on Tuesday.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Literally 'third day of the week' (երեք = three, շաբթի = week/Saturday).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A wreck shabby on Tuesday.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա երիտասարդ և խելացի է։ [He/She is young and smart.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Can also be used as a noun meaning 'a young person'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The young man ate a sardine.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս ճանապարհը շատ երկար է։ [This road is very long.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): That's a long air car.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նրա աչքերը երկնագույն են։ [His/Her eyes are light blue.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Compound word: երկինք (erkinq' - sky) + գույն (guyn - color). Literally 'sky-colored'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Light blue is the heir-can-go-in color for the royal baby's room.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես երկու տոմս եմ ուզում։ [I want two tickets.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Cardinal number.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I have 2 kangaroos that say Er-koo!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երկուշաբթի աշխատանքի եմ գնում։ [I go to work on Monday.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Literally 'second day of the week' (երկու = two, շաբթի = week/Saturday).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): On Monday, I saw air-coo-shabby pigeons.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես շատ երջանիկ եմ, որ տեսնում եմ քեզ։ [I am very happy to see you.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. The root word is related to երջանկություն (erjankut’yun), which means 'happiness'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): An 'air janitor' named Nick (Er-ja-nik) is always happy while cleaning the sky.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Պետք է զանգեմ մայրիկիս։ [I have to call my mom.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: զանգել (zangel). Imperative: զանգի՛ր (zangir). The root is 'զանգ' (zang), which means 'bell'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): To call her, he 'sang Elle' a song over the phone.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նրանք երկու զավակ ունեն՝ một տղա և մի աղջիկ։ [They have two children: a boy and a girl.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: զավակը (zavakə). Plural: զավակներ (zavakner). It is a slightly more formal term for a child than երեխա (yerekha).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The child is playing with a 'Z-vac'uum cleaner.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես զարմացած էի նրա նվերից։ [I was surprised by his gift.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Participle/Adjective from the verb զարմանալ (zarmanal), 'to be surprised'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I was surprised when I saw 'Zarma's cats' all wearing hats.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ զարմիկը ինձնից երկու տարով մեծ է։ [My cousin is two years older than me.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: զարմիկը (zarmikə). The female form is զարմուհի (zarmuhi).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My cousin Nick drives a 'Zar' (a car). He's 'Zar-Nick'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ զարմուհին շատ լավ է նկարում։ [My cousin paints very well.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: զարմուհին (zarmuhin). The suffix -ուհի (-uhi) indicates female, like in ուսուցչուհի (usuts'chuhi - female teacher).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My cousin goes 'hee hee' while driving her 'Zar-Moo'ving van.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այսօր աշխատանքի վայրում շատ զբաղված օր էր։ [Today was a very busy day at work.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Participle/Adjective from the verb զբաղվել (zbaghvel), 'to be occupied with'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'm too busy to clean this 'Z-bug vat'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Կներեք, որտե՞ղ է զուգարանը։ [Excuse me, where is the toilet?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: զուգարանը (zugaranə).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A 'zoo car ran' into the toilet building.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ջերմաստիճանը զրո աստիճան է։ [The temperature is zero degrees.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Number/Noun. An international word, very similar to 'zero'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A superhero named 'Z-Ro' has the power to make things disappear.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս գիրքը բավականին էժան է։ [This book is quite cheap.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. The opposite of թանկ (t'ank) - 'expensive'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Hey, Jean!', this is so cheap!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Շուկայում մի կիլոգրամ էժան տանձ գնեցի։ [I bought a kilogram of cheap pears at the market.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun phrase. էժան (ezhan) 'cheap' is an adjective modifying տանձ (tandz) 'pear'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Imagine 'Eugene dancing' because he found such cheap pears.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Առավոտյան վարժությունները ինձ էներգիա են տալիս։ [Morning exercises give me energy.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. An international word. Definite form: էներգիան (energian).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It sounds almost identical to 'energy'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Գյուղում մի ծերունի իր էշով գնում էր դաշտ։ [In the village, an old man was going to the field with his donkey.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: էշը (eshə). Plural: էշեր (esher).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The donkey made a mess, leaving a pile of 'ash'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա իմ մանկության ընկերն է։ [He is my childhood friend.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: ընկերը (enkerə). Female friend: ընկերուհի (enkeruhi).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My friend works at an 'ink air' factory, making ink out of thin air.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ ընտանիքը շատ մեծ է։ [My family is very large.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: ընտանիքը (entanik'ə).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My entire family is having a picnic on an 'ant-hill'. (@nt-anik)

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Դժվար է ընտրել այս երկու գույներից։ [It's hard to choose between these two colors.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: ընտրել (@ntrel). Imperative: ընտրի՛ր (@ntrir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I need to choose which 'ant' can 'drill' the best. (@nt-rel)

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս ռեստորանը շատ թանկ է։ [This restaurant is very expensive.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Opposite of էժան (ezhan) - 'cheap'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): That military 'tank' must be very expensive.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ թարգմանել այս բառը։ [Please translate this word.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: թարգմանել (t'argmanel). Imperative: թարգմանի՛ր (t'argmanir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): To translate, I have a 'target man L'—a man with an L on his shirt who helps me.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սիրում եմ թարմ մրգեր ուտել։ [I like to eat fresh fruits.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Can be used for food, air, news, etc.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The fresh produce is delivered right to 'the arm' of the house.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Սո՞ւրճ կխմես, թե՞ թեյ։ [Will you drink coffee or tea?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Conjunction. Used to present choices, often in questions (թե՞). It sounds similar to թեյ (t'ey), which means 'tea'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It's easy! Do you want coffee 'or' (թե) 'tea' (թեյ)?

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Առավոտյան սիրում եմ թեյ խմել։ [I like to drink tea in the morning.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: թեյ (t'ey). Definite form: թեյը (t'ey@). An international loanword, making it easy to remember.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I "T'EY"-ke my tea with lemon.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Դու թենիս խաղո՞ւմ ես։ [Do you play tennis?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: թենիս (t'enis). Definite form: թենիսը (t'enis@). A direct borrowing from the international word for 'tennis'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): This "T'ENIS" racket is a menace.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Տատիկս արդեն թոշակի է անցել։ [My grandmother has already retired (gone on pension).]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: թոշակ (t'oshak). Definite form: թոշակը (t'oshak@).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He "T'OSHAK" the pension check and ran away.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այգում շատ թոշակառուներ կան։ [There are many retirees in the park.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound word: թոշակ (t'oshak - pension) + առու (arru - taker). A person who takes a pension. Plural form: թոշակառուներ (t'oshakarruner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A "T'OSHAK-ARRRU" (retiree) is like a pirate saying "Arrrr, you took my pension!"

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա իր թոռան հետ զբոսնում է։ [She is walking with her grandchild.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: թոռ (t'orr). Plural: թոռներ (t'orrner). Definite form: թոռը (t'orr@).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The grandchild "T'ORR" the new curtains.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ինձ մի թերթ թուղթ կտա՞ս։ [Will you give me a sheet of paper?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: թուղթ (t'ught'). Definite form: թուղթը (t'ught'@).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I "T'UGHT'" (thought) I had some paper, but I was wrong.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ֆիլմը տևում է երկու ժամ։ [The movie lasts for two hours.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: ժամ (zham). Plural: ժամեր (zhamer). Also used to ask the time: Ժամը քանի՞սն է։ (Zham@ k'anisn e?) - What time is it?
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I can eat a whole jar of "ZHAM" (jam) in one hour.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ ժամացույցը հետ է մնում։ [My watch is slow.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound word: ժամ (zham - hour) + ցույց (ts'uyts' - to show). Literally an 'hour-shower'. Definite form: ժամացույցը (zhamats'uyts'@).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My "ZHAM-ATS'UYTS'" shows it's time to put jam on suits.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այսօր ի՞նչ ես անելու։ [What are you going to do today?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: ի (i). An interrogative pronoun. The base for many other question words like ինչու (why) and ինչպես (how).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): "INCH'" your way closer, I can't hear "what" you said.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Սա իմ գիրքն է։ [This is my book.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: ի (i). Possessive pronoun, first person singular.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It's all for 'IM'e, it's 'my' stuff!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես ինը տարեկան եմ։ [I am nine years old.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Cardinal number.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Let's meet at the hotel "INN@" nine o'clock.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հայրս ինժեներ է աշխատում։ [My father works as an engineer.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: ինժեներ (inzhener). A loanword from French via Russian. Definite form: ինժեները (inzhener@).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The "INZHENER" works on the 'engine here'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ինչո՞ւ ես ուշացել։ [Why are you late?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Interrogative adverb. Derived from ինչ (inch' - what).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): "INCH'U" (why) did you put an inch of goo on my shoe?

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ինչպե՞ս ես։ [How are you?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Interrogative adverb. Derived from ինչ (inch' - what) + պես (pes - like). Literally means 'like what?'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): "INCH'PES" do you get rid of these inch-pests? How?

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ինչպիսի՞ երաժշտություն ես սիրում։ [What kind of music do you like?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Interrogative adjective. Derived from ինչ (inch' - what) + պիսի (pisi - type/kind).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): "INCHPISI" pizza is this? What kind of fishy pizza is it?

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս գիրքը ինչքա՞ն արժե։ [How much does this book cost?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Interrogative adverb. Derived from ինչ (inch' - what) + քան (k'an - amount).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): "INCHK'AN" you really carry? How much?

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ինքը շատ լավ է երգում։ [He/she sings very well.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Personal pronoun, third person singular. Can mean 'he', 'she', or 'it', and is also used reflexively for 'himself/herself'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He put the "INK'@" on himself.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես վաղը ինքնաթիռով մեկնում եմ Փարիզ։ [Tomorrow I'm leaving for Paris by airplane.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound word: ինքն (ink'n - self) + թիռ (t'irr - from 'to fly'). It's a 'self-flyer'. Definite form: ինքնաթիռը (ink'nat'irr@).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The "INK'NAT'IRR" (airplane) flies itself with ink and tears.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սիրում եմ սուրճ, իսկ դո՞ւ։ [I like coffee, and you?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): A conjunction used to show contrast ('but', 'while') or to connect a follow-up question ('and').
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): There "ISK" a risk, but we must proceed.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա վերցրեց իր գիրքը։ [He/She took his/her (own) book.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Possessive pronoun, third person singular reflexive. It refers back to the subject of the sentence, meaning 'one's own'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He put the book on "IR" (his own) ear.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ քույրը ուզում է իրավաբան դառնալ։ [My sister wants to become a lawyer.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound noun: իրավունք (right/law) + -աբան (suffix for professions). Definite form: իրավաբանը (iravabany).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The lawyer said, 'You have a ban on entering!'

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Բոլորը խոսելու իրավունք ունեն։ [Everyone has the right to speak.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: իրավ- (irav-). Definite form: իրավունքը (iravunk'y). The basis for 'lawyer' (իրավաբան).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): You have oonk-ly one 'right' to choose.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նրանք սիրում են իրենց նոր տունը։ [They love their new house.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Possessive pronoun, 3rd person plural. Refers back to the subject of the sentence ('their own').
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Their rents are too high for their own good.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, միացրու լամպը, մութ է։ [Please turn on the lamp, it's dark.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): International borrowing. Definite form: լամպը (lampy).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Sounds exactly like the English word 'lamp'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ինչպե՞ս ես։ - Լավ եմ, շնորհակալություն։ [How are you? - I'm well, thank you.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective/Adverb. One of the most common and fundamental words in Armenian.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I 'love' feeling this good.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Տատիկը թոնրում համեղ լավաշ է թխում։ [Grandma bakes delicious lavash in the tonir.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. A traditional Armenian flatbread. Definite form: լավաշը (lavashy).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Lavish' the table with lots of 'lavash'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երեխան գիշերը սկսեց լացել։ [The child started to cry at night.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: լացել (lats'el). Imperative: լաց (lats').
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He started to cry over 'lots el'se.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սիրում եմ թեյ լիմոնով ու շաքարով։ [I like tea with lemon and sugar.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): International borrowing. Definite form: լիմոնը (limony).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Sounds almost exactly like the English word 'lemon'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երբ մեծանամ, ուզում եմ բժիշկ լինել։ [When I grow up, I want to be a doctor.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Irregular verb. Infinitive: լինել (linel). Imperative: եղի՛ր (yeghir). A fundamental verb.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): To be or not to be, that's the 'line L'iam drew.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ինձ խանութից մեկ լիտր կաթ է պետք։ [I need one liter of milk from the store.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): International borrowing, unit of measurement. Definite form: լիտրը (litry).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Sounds exactly like the English word 'liter'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հայկական ամառային լոլիկը շատ համեղ է։ [Armenian summer tomatoes are very tasty.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: լոլիկը (loliky). Plural: լոլիկներ (lolikner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Lola, lick the tomato juice off your chin.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ամռանը մենք գնում ենք լողալու Սևանում։ [In the summer, we go to swim in Lake Sevan.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: լողալ (loghal). Imperative: լողա՛ (logha).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): To swim, you must 'log all' your laps.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Լողասենյակը երկրորդ հարկում է։ [The bathroom is on the second floor.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound noun: լողալ (to swim/bathe) + սենյակ (room). Definite form: լողասենյակը (loghasenyaky).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): In the bathroom, a 'log' and a 'snake' are having a chat.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սիրում եմ դասական երաժշտություն լսել։ [I love to listen to classical music.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: լսել (lsel). Imperative: լսի՛ր (lsir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): To listen, you must 'sell' your silence.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Պետք է լվանալ ձեռքերը ուտելուց առաջ։ [You must wash your hands before eating.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: լվանալ (lvanal). Imperative: լվա՛ (lva).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I need to wash the 'lava null'ifying my car's paint job.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Լվացված շորերը չորանում են արևի տակ։ [The washed clothes are drying in the sun.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Past participle of լվանալ (to wash), used as an adjective.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The washed clothes are in 'lots of vats' ready to be dried.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Լրագրողը թերթի համար հոդված է գրում։ [The journalist is writing an article for the newspaper.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound noun: լուր (news) + գրող (writer). Definite form: լրագրողը (lragroghy).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A journalist will 'lie grog'-gy in the morning to get the early news.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երեխաները բակում խաղում են։ [The children are playing in the yard.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: խաղալ (khaghal). Imperative: խաղա՛ (khagha).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): That 'gal' can really play! She's tough.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս հայկական խաղողը շատ քաղցր է։ [This Armenian grape is very sweet.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: խաղողը (khaghoghy). Sounds similar to խաղալ (to play).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Don't eat the grape, it will make you 'cough all' night.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես խանութ եմ գնում հաց գնելու։ [I'm going to the shop to buy bread.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: խանութը (khanut'y).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): You 'cannot' enter the shop without a mask.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես ամեն օր շատ ջուր եմ խմում։ [I drink a lot of water every day.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Infinitive: խմել (khmel). Imperative (you, singular): խմի՛ր (khmir). The root is խմ- (khm-).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I could *khmel* (smell) the drink from across the room.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: - Շնորհակալություն։ - Խնդրեմ։ [- Thank you. - You're welcome.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): From the verb խնդրել (khndrel) - to ask. It's the first-person present tense form, literally 'I ask'. Used as a set phrase for 'you're welcome' or a polite 'please'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): You're welcome! You don't have to *khndrem* (condemn) yourself with gratitude.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, ինձ մի բաժակ սուրճ տվեք։ [Please, give me a cup of coffee.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Literally 'I am asking'. A very common and polite way to say 'please'. From the verb խնդրել (khndrel).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Please, *khndrum em* (I'm drumming) on the table for some service!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սիրում եմ ուտել հյութալի, կարմիր խնձոր։ [I love to eat juicy, red apples.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: խնձոր (khndzor). Definite form: խնձորը (khndzoruh). Plural: խնձորներ (khndzorner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): An apple a day keeps the huge *khndzor* (condor) away.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մայրիկս համեղ տորթ է թխում խոհանոցում։ [My mom is baking a delicious cake in the kitchen.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: խոհ (khoh) meaning 'thought' or 'culinary art'. The suffix -անոց (-anots') indicates a place. Definite form: խոհանոցը (khohanots'uh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The *co-host notes* for the cooking show are all over the kitchen.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա ազատ խոսում է երեք լեզվով։ [She speaks three languages fluently.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Infinitive: խոսել (khosel). Imperative (you, singular): խոսի՛ր (khosir). Root: խոս- (khos-).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): To speak, you have to get *khosel* (closer) to people.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Գարնանը խոտը կանաչում է։ [In the spring, the grass turns green.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: խոտ (khot). Definite form: խոտը (khotuh). Can also mean 'hay'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The grass was so long it got *khot* (caught) in the lawnmower.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա ինձ մի գեղեցիկ ծաղիկ նվիրեց։ [He gave me a beautiful flower.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: ծաղիկ (tsaghik). Definite form: ծաղիկը (tsaghikuh). Plural: ծաղիկներ (tsaghikner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): That flower has a *tsaghik* (psychic) ability to cheer people up.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս արկղը շատ ծանր է, չեմ կարող բարձրացնել։ [This box is very heavy, I can't lift it.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): An adjective. Root: ծանր (tsanr). Can also mean 'difficult' or 'serious'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): This is so heavy, my back is *tsanr* (sore).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այգում մի մեծ, հին ծառ կա։ [There is a big, old tree in the garden.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: ծառ (tsarr). Definite form: ծառը (tsarruh). Plural: ծառեր (tsarrer).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The tree is covered in sticky *tsarr* (tar).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես ծարավ եմ, մի բաժակ ջուր կտա՞ս։ [I am thirsty, will you give me a glass of water?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): An adjective describing a state. Often used with the verb 'to be' (եմ, ես, է...).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'm so thirsty, I could drink an entire *tsar-av* (jar of) water.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ տատիկն ու պապիկը ծեր են, բայց շատ ակտիվ։ [My grandmother and grandfather are old, but very active.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective used for people. For objects, 'հին' (hin) is used. Root: ծեր (tser).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The old man said, 'Yes *tser* (sir), I remember everything.'

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հայաստանը հայտնի է իր քաղցր ծիրանով։ [Armenia is famous for its sweet apricot.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: ծիրան (tsiran). The apricot is a national symbol of Armenia. Definite form: ծիրանը (tsiranuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The apricot *tsi-ran* (she ran) away with the spoon.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այստեղ ծխելը խստիվ արգելվում է։ [Smoking is strictly forbidden here.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Infinitive: ծխել (tskhel). Imperative (you, singular): ծխի՛ր (tskhir). Root: ծուխ (tsukh) - smoke.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): To smoke is to put your health in a *tsk-hel* (hell).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մի՛ եղիր ծույլ, արի՛ գնանք զբոսնելու։ [Don't be lazy, let's go for a walk.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): An adjective. Root: ծույլ (tsuyl).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The lazy person says, 'It's *tsuyl* (too) late to do anything.'

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ ընկերը ծրագրավորող է աշխատում։ [My friend works as a programmer.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): A compound noun from ծրագիր (tsragir - program) + ավորող (avorrogh - one who does). Definite form: ծրագրավորողը (tsragravorroghuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A programmer's life: *tsragir-avor-rogh* (tragic or raw)?

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երեխան ամեն առավոտ կաթ է խմում։ [The child drinks milk every morning.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: կաթ (kat'). Definite form: կաթը (kat'uh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I need some milk for my *kat'* (cat).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ցուրտ օրերին ես սիրում եմ տաք կակաո խմել։ [On cold days I like to drink hot cocoa.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): An international word, spelled phonetically in Armenian. It is a loanword.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): This one is easy! *Kakao* sounds just like cocoa.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Դու թե՞յ ես ուզում, կամ սուրճ։ [Do you want tea or coffee?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): A conjunction used to present alternatives. Root: կամ (kam).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Be *kam* (calm) and choose one or the other.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Կամաց քայլիր, հատակը սայթաքուն է։ [Walk slowly, the floor is slippery.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): An adverb. Can mean 'slowly' or 'quietly' depending on context. Root: կամաց (kamats).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Drive slowly and be quiet. Use your *kamats* (calm arts).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ինչպե՞ս են գործերդ։ - Կամաց-կամաց։ [How are things? - So-so.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb. A reduplication of կամաց (kamats), which means 'slowly'. The repetition gives it the meaning of 'getting by' or 'so-so'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Things are going 'come on, come on' (kamats-kamats), just so-so.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Գարնանը բոլոր ծառերը կանաչ են։ [In the spring, all the trees are green.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: կանաչ (kanach').
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Imagine opening a 'can of nuts' (kanach') and they are all bright green.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, սպասեք հաջորդ կանգառում։ [Please, wait at the next stop.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. From the verb կանգնել (kangnel) - 'to stop/stand'. Definite form: կանգառը (kangaruh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A KANGARoo (kangar) is waiting for the bus at the stop.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, կապիր կոշիկներդ։ [Please, tie your shoes.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: կապել (kapel). Imperative: կապիր (kapir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Help me tie the 'cape L' (kapel) on the superhero.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երկինքը այսօր զարմանալի կապույտ է։ [The sky is surprisingly blue today.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: կապույտ (kapuyt).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I 'put' (kapuyt) on my blue captain's hat.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ կատուն սիրում է քնել արևի տակ։ [My cat loves to sleep in the sun.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: կատուն (katun). Plural: կատուներ (katuner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Be careful, that cat might 'cut you' (katu).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Առավոտյան սիրում եմ հաց ու կարագ ուտել։ [In the morning, I like to eat bread and butter.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: կարագը (karaguh). A mass noun.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I accidentally drove my 'car' on a 'rag' (karag) covered in butter.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սիրում եմ գիրք կարդալ քնելուց առաջ։ [I like to read a book before sleeping.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: կարդալ (kardal). Imperative: կարդա (karda).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I want to read the 'card all' (kardal) day long.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս պատմությունը շատ կարճ է։ [This story is very short.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: կարճ (karch). Can also be used as an adverb for 'briefly'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Because the trip was short, I took my 'car' to the 'church' (karch).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս խնձորը շատ կարմիր ու հյութալի է։ [This apple is very red and juicy.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: կարմիր (karmir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My 'car mirror' (karmir) is painted bright red.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Դու կարո՞ղ ես ինձ օգնել։ [Can you help me?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb (modal). Infinitive: կարողանալ (karoghanal). Commonly used in conjugated forms: կարող եմ (karogh em - I can), կարող ես (karogh es - you can), etc.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I 'can' do it, but the 'car can go all' (karoghanal) day to get there.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես շատ եմ կարոտում ընտանիքիս, երբ հեռու եմ։ [I miss my family a lot when I am far away.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: կարոտել (karotel). The object being missed is in the dative case.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I miss staying at that 'car hotel' (karotel).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երեկոյան ընթրիքի համար տապակած կարտոֆիլ ենք ուտելու։ [We are going to eat fried potatoes for dinner tonight.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: կարտոֆիլը (kartofiluh). A clear loanword from Russian/German.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I need to 'fill' my shopping 'cart' (kartofil) with potatoes.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Վաղը ընկերներիս հետ կաֆե ենք գնալու։ [Tomorrow we are going to a cafe with my friends.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. International loanword. Definite form: կաֆեն (kafen).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Let's meet at the 'cafe' for a coffee. (It sounds almost the same!).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, ինձ մի կես բաժակ ջուր տվեք։ [Please, give me half a glass of water.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun/Adjective. Root: կես (kes). Can modify a noun or stand alone.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): She gave me half the cake and a 'kiss' (kes) on the cheek.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Կեսօրին արևը ամենաշատն է տաքացնում։ [At noon, the sun is the hottest.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Compound word from կես (kes - half) + օր (or - day). Literally 'half-day'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): At noon, you 'kiss ore' (kesor) that you found in the mine.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խանութից երկու կիլոգրամ լոլիկ գնեցի։ [I bought two kilograms of tomatoes from the store.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. International loanword. Definite form: կիլոգրամը (kilogramuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): This golden 'key' weighs a 'kilogram'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նրա կինը շատ խելացի կին է։ [His wife is a very smart woman.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Can mean 'wife' or 'woman'. Definite form: կինը (kinuh). Irregular plural: կանայք (kanayk').
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My 'kin' (kin) includes my wife and all the women in my family.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Եկեք այս խնձորը երկու մասի կիսենք։ [Let's share this apple in two.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Derived from կես (kes) 'half'. Infinitive: կիսել (kisel). Imperative: կիսիր (kisir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Let's 'kiss L' (kisel) shaped chocolates and share them.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Կիրակի օրը ես սովորաբար հանգստանում եմ տանը։ [On Sunday, I usually rest at home.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. From Greek κυριακή (kyriakí), 'Lord's day'. Definite form: կիրակին (kirakin).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): On Sunday, I clean the house with a 'key' and a 'raggy' (kiraki) cloth.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Լավ, վաղը կհանդիպենք։ [Okay, we'll meet tomorrow.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Future tense, 1st person plural of հանդիպել (handipel - to meet). Imperative: հանդիպի՛ր (handipir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): KHANDIPENK' sounds like "Can't depend" on seeing you soon, but I hope we will!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Արամ Խաչատրյանը հայտնի կոմպոզիտոր է։ [Aram Khachaturian is a famous composer.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. A cognate of the English word. Definite form: կոմպոզիտորը (kompozitoruh). Plural: կոմպոզիտորներ (kompozitorner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A KOMPOZITOR is a composer. It sounds almost identical!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս նոր կոշիկները շատ հարմարավետ են։ [These new shoes are very comfortable.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: կոշիկ (koshik). Definite form: կոշիկը (koshikuh). Common Plural: կոշիկներ (koshikner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My KOSHIK gave me a "cash kick" because it was so expensive.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Գյուղում մի սպիտակ կով տեսա։ [I saw a white cow in the village.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: կով (kov). Definite form: կովը (kovuh). Plural: կովեր (kover).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The KOV is hiding in the "cove" by the sea.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Շատ համեղ էր, ես արդեն կուշտ եմ։ [It was very delicious, I'm already full.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: կուշտ (kusht). Describes the state of being full/satiated.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'm so full, I need to be "cushed" onto the sofa.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ այս թուղթը երկու մասի կտրել։ [Please cut this paper in two.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: կտրել (ktrel). Imperative (singular): կտրի՛ր (ktrir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I need to KTREL ("cut-rell") a roll of paper.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ձմռանը մենք վառարանի կրակով էինք տաքանում։ [In winter, we warmed ourselves with the fire from the stove.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: կրակ (krak). Definite form: կրակը (krakuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Watch out for the fire, it will KRAK ("crack") the logs.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Կարո՞ղ եք խնդրում եմ ևս մեկ անգամ կրկնել։ [Can you please repeat one more time?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: կրկնել (krknel). Imperative (singular): կրկնի՛ր (krknir). The root կրկին (krkin) means 'again'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The parrot in the kirk will KRKNEL ("kirk-nell") everything you say.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Գլխացավի համար հաբ պետք է խմեմ։ [I need to take a pill for my headache.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: հաբ (hab). Definite form: հաբը (habuh). Plural: հաբեր (haber).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It's a bad HAB-it to take too many pills.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ամառային հագուստը պահարանում է։ [The summer clothes are in the closet.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Collective noun derived from the verb հագնել (hagnel) - to wear. Definite form: հագուստը (hagustuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A "gust" of wind blew my HAGUST (clothes) off the line.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս գիրքը հազար դրամ արժե։ [This book costs one thousand dram.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Number. Root: հազար (hazar).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It would be a HAZAR-d to count to 1000 all at once.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես շատ հազվադեպ եմ կինո գնում։ [I go to the cinema very rarely.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb. A compound word from հազիվ (haziv - barely) and դեպք (depk' - case).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): You will rarely see someone who "HAS-VA-DEEP" pockets like that.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա հաճախ է զանգում իր տատիկին։ [He often calls his grandmother.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb. Root: հաճախ (hachakh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He often laughs so hard he starts to HACHAKH ("ha-choke").

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սովորում եմ Երևանի պետական համալսարանում։ [I study at Yerevan State University.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Compound word: համա- (hama-, 'all') + լսարան (lsaran, 'auditorium'). Definite form: համալսարանը (hamalsaranuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): "HAM-ALL-SARAN" to the university to get her degree.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ համակարգիչը շատ հին է։ [My computer is very old.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Compound word: համա- (hama-, 'all') + կարգ (karg, 'order') + -իչ (-ich, suffix for agent/tool). Literally 'system-izer'. Definite form: համակարգիչը (hamakargichuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My computer is broken, I'll fix it with a HAMAKARGICH' ("hammer-car-kick").

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս նվերը քո համար է։ [This gift is for you.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Postposition. It comes after the noun/pronoun it modifies.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I bought this HAMAR ("hammer") for you.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ ընկերը հայ է։ [My friend is Armenian.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun/Adjective. The root name for an Armenian person, from the legendary patriarch Hayk. Plural: հայեր (hayer).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The Armenian person waved and said "HAY!"

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հայաստանը շատ գեղեցիկ երկիր է։ [Armenia is a very beautiful country.]
