Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: հայ (hay) + -ստան (-stan, a common suffix for 'land of'). So, 'Land of the Armenians'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Armenia is a HAYASTAN ("high-a-stan"), with many tall mountains.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես ուզում եմ սովորել հայերեն։ [I want to learn Armenian.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun/Adverb. Root: հայ (hay) + -երեն (-eren, suffix for languages). Can mean 'the Armenian language' or 'in Armenian'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The Armenian language sounds like "HAY-AIR-IN" my ears.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սիրում եմ հայկական խոհանոցը։ [I love Armenian cuisine.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: հայ (hay) + -ական (-akan, adjectival suffix). Used to describe something as being Armenian.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): This Armenian coffee is so strong it's a HAYKAKAN ("high-kickin'") experience.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հայրիկս ամեն օր ինձ տանում է դպրոց։ [My father takes me to school every day.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: հայր (hayr - father). '-իկ' (-ik) is a diminutive suffix, making it more affectionate like 'daddy'. Definite form: հայրիկը (hayrikə).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My dad says "HI, RICK!" every morning when he sees his friend Rick.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, հանգիստ եղիր, ամեն ինչ լավ կլինի։ [Please, be calm, everything will be fine.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Can also mean 'rest' or 'quiet'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He HUNG a GHOST on the wall, but he remained surprisingly calm about it.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես միշտ մարզասրահում հանքային ջուր եմ խմում։ [I always drink mineral water at the gym.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound noun. հանք (hank') means 'mine' + '-ային' (-ayin) adjectival suffix + ջուր (jur) 'water'. Literally 'mine-related water'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The miners are HANKERIN' for some JEWELRY-grade water.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հաջողություն քննությանդ։ [Good luck on your exam.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. From the verb հաջողել (hajoghel) - to succeed. '-ություն' (-ut'yun) is a suffix that creates abstract nouns.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): To wish good luck, you HAVE TO JOG with a TUNE on.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ձմռանը ես հաստ շարֆ եմ կրում։ [In winter I wear a thick scarf.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. The opposite is բարակ (barak) - thin.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): That book is so thick, it HAS T-o be heavy.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Տատիկս գյուղում շատ հավեր ունի։ [My grandmother has many hens in the village.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Plural: հավեր (haver). Often used for 'chicken' in a culinary context as well.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I HAVE a hen in my backyard.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա մի քիչ հարբած էր երեկույթին։ [He was a little drunk at the party.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Past participle used as an adjective, from the verb հարբել (harbel) - to get drunk.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He was so drunk, he was hitting on a HARP BAT.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Չարժի այդքան շատ գինի խմել, կարող ես հարբել։ [It's not worth drinking that much wine, you might get drunk.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: հարբել (harbel). Imperative: հարբի՛ր (harbir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He likes to get drunk and ring the HARP BELL.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս բազկաթոռը շատ հարմար է։ [This armchair is very comfortable.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Can be used for physical comfort or convenience of a situation.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): This comfortable chair is HARD to MAR (damage).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես հարյուր դրամով գրիչ գնեցի։ [I bought a pen for one hundred dram.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Numeral. Forms the base for other hundreds: երկու հարյուր (yerku haryur - 200).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I have to HURRY, YOUR honor, I'm late by 100 minutes!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես մի հարց ունեմ ձեզ։ [I have a question for you.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: հարցը (harts'ə). Plural: հարցեր (harts'er).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): That's a HARD question.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մի՛ վախեցիր ուսուցչիդ հարցնել։ [Don't be afraid to ask your teacher.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: հարցնել (harts'nel). Imperative: հարցրո՛ւ (harts'ru). Derived from the noun հարց (harts') - question.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): HARTS'-NELL is the person you need to ask.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մեր նոր հարևանը շատ բարի մարդ է։ [Our new neighbor is a very kind person.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: հարևանը (harevanə). Plural: հարևաններ (harevanner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My neighbor drives a HARE VAN.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հայկական լավաշը շատ համեղ հաց է։ [Armenian lavash is a very tasty bread.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: հացը (hats'ə). Can also refer to 'a meal' in a broader sense.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I love bread and HATS.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ հեռախոսի մարտկոցը նստել է։ [My phone's battery is dead.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound noun. From հեռու (herru - far) + խոսել (khosel - to speak). Literally 'far-speaker'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My HAIRY COUSIN is always on the phone.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խանութը այստեղից շատ հեռու է։ [The store is very far from here.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb/Adjective. The opposite is մոտիկ (motik) - near.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): She threw the ball from HERE to YOU, which is very far away.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Սա շատ հետաքրքիր գիրք է։ [This is a very interesting book.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Derived from հետ (het - behind) and քրքրել (k'rk'rel - to rummage/dig).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I HATE A CRACKER, which is an interesting opinion to have.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նախ դասերս կանեմ, հետո կգնամ զբոսնելու։ [First I will do my homework, then I will go for a walk.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb. Often used to sequence events. Can be combined with the ablative case: դասից հետո (dasits' heto) - after the lesson.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'll see you after, HEY TOE!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես հիասթափված եմ իմ արդյունքներից։ [I am disappointed with my results.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Past participle used as an adjective, from the verb հիասթափվել (hiast'ap'vel) - to be disappointed.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He was disappointed when HE ATE UP VATS of soap instead of ice cream.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես պետք է հիմա գնամ տուն։ [I have to go home now.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb of time. Very common and fundamental word.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): "HE, MA!" come here NOW!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ պապիկի տունը շատ հին է, բայց շատ գեղեցիկ։ [My grandpa's house is very old, but very beautiful.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: հին (hin). It describes nouns, usually preceding them.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My old car has a squeaky 'hin'ge.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, ինձ հինգ խնձոր տվեք։ [Please, give me five apples.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Cardinal number. Root: հինգ (hing).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I 'hing' my coat on one of the 'five' hooks.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հինգշաբթի օրը ես ընկերներիս հետ եմ հանդիպում։ [On Thursday I meet with my friends.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Compound of հինգ (hing - five) + շաբաթ (shabat' - week). Definite form: հինգշաբթին (hingshabt'in).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): On Thursday, I 'hinge' the door 'shabby'ly 't'o annoy my neighbor.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես միշտ հիշում եմ իմ մանկությունը։ [I always remember my childhood.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: հիշել (hishel). Imperative: հիշի՛ր (hishir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I try to 'remember' who 'he shell'ed the peanuts for.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս գիրքը հիսուն էջ ունի։ [This book has fifty pages.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Cardinal number. Root: հիսուն (hisun).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He soon' will be '50' years old.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Եթե հիվանդ ես, պետք է տանը մնաս։ [If you are sick, you must stay home.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective/Noun. Can mean 'sick' or 'a patient'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He vanned' the 'sick' patient away in an ambulance.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Աշնանը մարդիկ հաճախ են հիվանդանում։ [In autumn, people often get sick.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: հիվանդանալ (hivandanal). Imperative: հիվանդացի՛ր (hivandats'ir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He-vanned-an-all' the people who were starting to 'get sick'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Բժիշկը աշխատում է հիվանդանոցում։ [The doctor works at the hospital.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Compound of հիվանդ (hivand - patient) + -անոց (-anots' - place of). Definite: հիվանդանոցը (hivandanots'y).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The 'hospital' has a 'he-vanned-a-gnats' problem.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այսօր երեկոյան մենք հյուրեր ունենք։ [Tonight we have guests.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Plural form: հյուրեր (hyurer).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Your' 'guest' has arrived.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մենք կմնանք քաղաքի կենտրոնում գտնվող հյուրանոցում։ [We will stay at a hotel in the city center.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Compound of հյուր (hyur - guest) + -անոց (-anots' - place of). Definite: հյուրանոցը (hyuranots'y).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): There's a 'hotel' just for 'your gnats'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ընտանիքը հավաքվել է հյուրասենյակում և հեռուստացույց է դիտում։ [The family has gathered in the sitting room and is watching TV.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Compound of հյուր (hyur - guest) + սենյակ (senyak - room). Definite: հյուրասենյակը (hyurasenyaky).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Your sen-yak' is waiting for you in the 'sitting room'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ամբողջ օրը աշխատել եմ, շատ հոգնած եմ։ [I worked all day, I am very tired.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective (Past Participle from հոգնել - to get tired).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'm so 'tired' I could eat a bucket of 'hog nuts'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հոկտեմբերին եղանակը սկսում է ցրտել։ [In October the weather starts to get cold.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. International word. Definite form: հոկտեմբերը (hoktembery).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Hulk-timber!' he shouted, chopping wood in 'October'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս հողը շատ բերրի է։ [This soil is very fertile.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: հողը (hoghy).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A happy 'hog' rolls in the 'soil'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ հորաքույրը շատ համեղ տոլմա է պատրաստում։ [My aunt (father's sister) makes very delicious dolma.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Compound of հոր (hor - of father) + քույր (k'uyr - sister).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Imagine a 'horror choir' composed of all your father's sisters ('aunts').

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հորեղբայրս ինձ սովորեցրեց շախմատ խաղալ։ [My uncle (father's brother) taught me to play chess.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Compound of հոր (hor - of father) + եղբայր (eghbayr - brother).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The 'horror buyer' at the film studio is my 'uncle' (father's brother).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես շատ հուզված էի քո նվերից, շնորհակալություն։ [I was very touched by your gift, thank you.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective (Past Participle from հուզվել - to get excited/moved).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Who's vats' of tears? The emotionally 'touched' man.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մի՛ հուզվիր, ամեն ինչ լավ կլինի։ [Don't get excited/worried, everything will be fine.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: հուզվել (huzvel). Imperative: հուզվի՛ր (huzvir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Who's vel'vet cape is this? The person who 'got so moved' that they left it behind.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հուլիսին Հայաստանում շատ շոգ է լինում։ [In July it is very hot in Armenia.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. International word. Definite form: հուլիսը (hulisy).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Who is' this? It's my friend Julie, who was born in 'July'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հունիսին ծիրանը հասունանում է։ [In June, the apricots ripen.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. International word. Definite form: հունիսը (hunisy).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Who is' going on vacation in 'June'?

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հունվարին Երևանում սովորաբար ցուրտ է լինում։ [It's usually cold in Yerevan in January.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: հունվար (hunvar). Definite form: հունվարը (hunvaruh). This word is derived from the Latin 'Ianuarius', making it a cognate in many European languages.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): You only see the new HOON-VAR model car in January.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հուսով եմ, որ վաղը արևոտ օր կլինի։ [I hope tomorrow will be a sunny day.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: հույս (huys), meaning 'hope'. 'husov' is the instrumental case ('with hope') and 'em' means 'I am'. Literally, 'I am with hope'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): "WHO SO VEM-ently hopes for the best?" I do, husov em.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երեկոյան հանդիպենք Հանրապետության հրապարակում։ [Let's meet at Republic Square in the evening.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: հրապարակ (hraparak). Definite form: հրապարակը (hraparakuh). Used for city squares and public spaces.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Everyone is having a "HURRAH-PARADE" in the main square.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես ուզում եմ քեզ հրավիրել իմ ծննդյան օրին։ [I want to invite you to my birthday.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: հրավիրել (hravirel). Imperative: հրավիրի՛ր (hravirir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): To invite him, you'll HAVE TO REEL him in with a good offer.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս փողոցում պետք է թեքվել ձախ։ [On this street, you need to turn left.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: ձախ (dzakh). Adjective. It is the opposite of աջ (ach), meaning right.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The DUCK turned left.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նրա ձայնը շատ հաճելի է։ [Her voice is very pleasant.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: ձայն (dzayn). Definite form: ձայնը (dzaynuh). Common plural: ձայներ (dzayner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The DESIGN of her voice is beautiful.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Սա ձեր տոմսե՞րն են։ [Are these your tickets?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Possessive pronoun, 2nd person plural or formal. Use when speaking to more than one person or formally to one person. Compare with քո (k'o) - your (singular, informal).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): "DZER" is your house? (Pointing at a group of people).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սիրում եմ, երբ ձմռանը շատ ձյուն է գալիս։ [I love it when it snows a lot in winter.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: ձմեռ (dzmerr). Definite form: ձմեռը (dzmerruh). Genitive case (of winter) is ձմռան (dzmran).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): DIMMER the lights, it's getting dark early in the winter.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Առավոտյան ամբողջ քաղաքը ձյան տակ էր։ [In the morning, the whole city was under snow.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: ձյուն (dzyun). Definite form: ձյունը (dzyunuh). Notice the similarity to ձմեռ (dzmerr) - winter.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The snow fell on the sand DUNE.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Առավոտյան նախաճաշին սիրում եմ ձու ուտել։ [I like to eat an egg for breakfast in the morning.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: ձու (dzu). Definite form: ձուն (dzun). Plural: ձվեր (dzver).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I saw a giant egg at the ZOO.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Սևանա լճի իշխան ձուկը շատ համեղ է։ [The Ishkhan fish from Lake Sevan is very tasty.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: ձուկ (dzuk). Definite form: ձուկը (dzukuh). Plural: ձկներ (dzkner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A fish jumped out of the water and landed on the DUKE.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, երկու ձեռքով բռնիր ղեկը։ [Please, hold the steering wheel with two hands.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: ղեկ (ghek). Definite form: ղեկը (ghekuh). The sound 'ղ' (gh) is a voiced uvular fricative, similar to a French 'r'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The steering wheel feels like a GECK-o's skin.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մեր ղեկավարը շատ խելացի մարդ է։ [Our boss is a very smart person.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound word: ղեկ (ghek) 'steering wheel' + ավար (avar) 'ruler'. A person who 'steers'. Definite form: ղեկավարը (ghekavaruh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The boss (GHEKAVAR) will GET A CAR if we succeed.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես նրան միանգամից ճանաչեցի իր ձայնից։ [I recognized him immediately by his voice.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: ճանաչել (chanach'el). Imperative: ճանաչի՛ր (chanach'ir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): "CAN I CHELL you that I recognize you?" she asked.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս ճանապարհը տանում է դեպի Գառնու տաճար։ [This road leads to the Garni temple.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: ճանապարհ (chanaparh). Definite form: ճանապարհը (chanaparhuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): On the road, there was A CHANNAH-PARROT giving directions.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ժամը մեկին մենք միասին ճաշում ենք։ [At one o'clock, we have lunch together.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: ճաշ (chash). Definite form: ճաշը (chashuh). The verb 'to eat lunch' is ճաշել (chashel).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Let's CHASE down some food for lunch.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ընտանիքը հավաքվել էր ճաշասենյակում։ [The family had gathered in the dining room.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound word: ճաշ (chash) 'lunch' + սենյակ (senyak) 'room'. Literally 'lunch-room'. Definite form: ճաշասենյակը (chashasenyakuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A SHAGGY SNAKE is in our dining room!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս գեղեցիկ շենքի ճարտարապետը շատ տաղանդավոր է։ [The architect of this beautiful building is very talented.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: ճարտարապետ (chartarapet). Definite form: ճարտարապետը (chartarapetuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The architect drew a CHART of her PET on the blueprint.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մենք վաղը գնում ենք թատրոն, ճի՞շտ է։ [We're going to the theater tomorrow, right?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective/Adverb. The question particle 'է' (e) is often added to turn it into a question tag 'right?'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): SHE'S right, isn't she?

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Դա ճիշտ պատասխանն է։ [That is the right answer.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective/Adverb. Root: ճիշտ (chisht). The opposite of սխալ (skhal) 'wrong'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He hit the target right in the CHEST.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Փոքրիկ ճուտիկը վազում է իր մայրիկի հետևից։ [The little chick is running after its mother.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: ճուտ (ch'ut). Plural: ճտեր (ch'ter). The diminutive form is ճուտիկ (ch'utik).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): You have to 'CH'UT' the chick out of its shell.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նախաճաշին ես սիրում եմ մածուն ուտել մեղրով։ [For breakfast, I like to eat yogurt with honey.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: մածուն (matsun). Definite form: մածունը (matsunə). A traditional Armenian fermented milk product.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My 'MATS'UN' (mad son) loves to spill yogurt on the mat.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մայիսին ծաղիկները սկսում են ծաղկել։ [In May, the flowers start to bloom.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: մայիս (mayis). Definite form: մայիսը (mayisə). From Latin 'Maius'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I 'MAYIS' go on vacation in May.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ մայրիկը ամենահամեղ տոլման է պատրաստում։ [My mother makes the most delicious dolma.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: մայր (mayr - mother). '-իկ' (-ik) is a common diminutive/affectionate suffix. Definite form: մայրիկը (mayrikə).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My mother is named Mary, Rick. 'MAYRIK'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մանուկը քնած է իր օրորոցում։ [The baby is sleeping in his cradle.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: մանուկ (manuk). Definite form: մանուկը (manukə). Plural: մանուկներ (manukner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The baby is reading a 'MANUK'-script (manuscript).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա կրում էր գեղեցիկ մանուշակագույն զգեստ։ [She was wearing a beautiful purple dress.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Compound word: մանուշակ (manushak - violet flower) + գույն (guyn - color).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A 'MAN-USH-AKA-GUYN' (man who's a king) always wears a purple robe.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Դու ուզու՞մ ես մասնակցել խաղին։ [Do you want to participate in the game?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: մասնակցել (masnakts'el). Imperative: մասնակցի՛ր (masnakts'ir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'll participate if there's a 'MAS'NAK-TS'EL' (massive snack sale).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, ինձ մի մատիտ տուր։ [Please, give me a pencil.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: մատիտ (matit). Definite form: մատիտը (matitə). Plural: մատիտներ (matitner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Don't put the pencil on the 'MAT IT' will roll off.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես շաբաթը երեք անգամ գնում եմ մարզասրահ։ [I go to the gym three times a week.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Compound word: մարզ- (marz- from մարզել - to train) + սրահ (srah - hall). Definite form: մարզասրահը (marzasrahə).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I see 'MARZ-A-SRAH' (Mars, ah!) when I lift heavy weights at the gym.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մարտ ամսին գարունը սկսվում է։ [Spring begins in the month of March.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: մարտ (mart). Definite form: մարտը (martə). Also means 'battle'. From Latin 'Martius' (Mars, god of war).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The soldiers 'MART'-ch into battle in March.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երևանը Հայաստանի ամենամեծ քաղաքն է։ [Yerevan is the biggest city in Armenia.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: մեծ (mets). Comparative: ավելի մեծ (aveli mets). Superlative: ամենամեծ (amenamets).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I 'METS' a very big and great person today.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խանութից մեկ հաց գնիր։ [Buy one bread from the store.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Number. Root: մեկ (mek).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): MEK' a wish when you see one shooting star.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Գրքի առաջին մեկ բաժինը շատ հետաքրքիր էր։ [The first one section of the book was very interesting.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun phrase. մեկ (mek - one) + բաժին (bazhin - portion, section).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Give 'MEK BAZHIN' (my cousin) one section of the cake.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա շահեց մեկ միլիոն դրամ։ [He won one million drams.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun phrase. մեկ (mek - one) + միլիոն (milion - million).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I want to make 'MEK MILION' dollars. (Sounds very similar).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Գնացքը կմեկնի տասը րոպեից։ [The train will leave in ten minutes.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: մեկնել (meknel). Imperative: մեկնի՛ր (meknir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It's time to leave, so let's 'MEK'NEL' (make nail) down the hatches!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մենք ընկերներ ենք։ [We are friends.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Pronoun. First-person plural pronoun, nominative case.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): MENK'' (monks) are praying for all of us, we are one group.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս սենյակը երեք մետր երկարություն ունի։ [This room is three meters long.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: մետր (metr). Definite form: մետրը (metrə). An international word.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I 'MET'R' him a meter away from here.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երևանի մետրոն շատ հարմար է։ [The Yerevan metro is very convenient.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: մետրո (metro). Definite form: մետրոն (metron). An international word.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'll 'MET'RO' you at the metro station. (Sounds almost identical).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Սա մեր տունն է։ [This is our house.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Pronoun. First-person plural possessive pronoun.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): MER'-ry Christmas to all, this is our celebration!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես նոր մեքենա եմ ուզում գնել։ [I want to buy a new car.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: մեքենա (mek'ena). Definite form: մեքենան (mek'enan). From Greek 'mēkhanḗ'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My friend 'MEK'ENA' (McKenna) just bought a new car.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես ուզում եմ մի բան խմել։ [I want to drink something.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. Composed of 'մի' (mi - a/one) and 'բան' (ban - thing). Literally means 'a thing' or 'one thing'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): There is a 'MI BAN' on *something* on the counter.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, սպասիր մի րոպե։ [Please, wait one minute.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. Composed of 'մի' (mi - one) and 'րոպե' (rope - minute).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): To climb MI ROPE will take more than *one minute*.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես մի քիչ հայերեն եմ խոսում։ [I speak a little bit of Armenian.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. Composed of 'մի' (mi - a) and 'քիչ' (k'ich' - little). An idiomatic way to say 'a bit'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): There is *a little bit* of frosting on ME K'ICH'in.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Եկեք միասին ճաշենք։ [Let's have lunch together.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb. Root is 'մի' (mi), meaning 'one'. The suffix '-ասին' (-asin) conveys the idea of 'as one' or togetherness.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): M.I.A. will SING with us *together*.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս մեքենան արժե գրեթե մեկ միլիոն դրամ։ [This car costs almost one million dram.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. A cognate with the English word 'million', making it easy to remember.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It sounds exactly like the English word *million*.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Լավ, ես պետք է գնամ, մինչ վաղը։ [Okay, I have to go, see you tomorrow.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. Composed of 'մինչ' (minch' - until) and 'վաղը' (vagh@ - tomorrow). The literal meaning is 'until tomorrow'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I have to MINCE this VAGUE vegetable, *see you tomorrow*!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա միշտ օգնում է իր ընկերներին։ [He always helps his friends.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb of frequency.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I *always* MISHT my appointment when I oversleep.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես այսօր ընթրիքի համար միս եմ պատրաստում։ [I am cooking meat for dinner today.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: միսը (mis@).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I *miss* eating good *meat*.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ամռանը շուկայում շատ թարմ միրգ կա։ [In the summer, there is a lot of fresh fruit in the market.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: միրգը (mirg@). Plural: մրգեր (mrger).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The murky MIRG swamp grows strange *fruit*.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա որոշեց մնալ հյուրանոցում ևս երկու օր։ [She decided to stay at the hotel for two more days.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: մնալ (mnal). Imperative (you, singular): մնա՛ (mna՛).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): MA NAL-ed the door shut, so we have *to stay* here.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ամպերը մոխրագույն էին, և շուտով անձրև սկսվեց։ [The clouds were gray, and soon it started to rain.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Compound word from 'մոխիր' (mokhir - ash) + 'գույն' (guyn - color). Literally 'ash-color'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The MOKE-RA-GUYN monster's skin was the color of ash, which is *gray*.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ծննդյան տորթի վրա վառվում էր մեկ մոմ։ [One candle was burning on the birthday cake.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: մոմը (mom@). Plural: մոմեր (momer).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My MOM lit a *candle* for my birthday.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Դեղատունը գտնվում է հիվանդանոցի մոտ։ [The pharmacy is located near the hospital.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Postposition. Comes after the noun it modifies, which is typically in the genitive case (e.g., տան մոտ - near the house).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The castle *moat* is *near* the walls.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երբ երեխան տեսավ իր մայրիկին, նա սկսեց մոտենալ։ [When the child saw his mother, he started to approach.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Derived from 'մոտ' (mot - near). Infinitive: մոտենալ (motenal). Imperative (you, singular): մոտեցի՛ր (motets'ir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): MOT-EN-ALL the sheep started *to approach* the farmer for food.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ տունը քո տանը շատ մոտիկ է։ [My house is very close to your house.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective/Adverb. A variation of 'մոտ' (mot), often used to describe closeness in relationships or proximity.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): You have to MOW-TIK-s on the lawn that are *near* the house.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ մորաքույրը շատ համեղ տոլմա է պատրաստում։ [My aunt makes very delicious dolma.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Compound word: 'մոր' (mor - mother's) + 'քույր' (k'uyr - sister). Literally 'mother's sister'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It's MOR-A-K'UYR-ious how my *aunt* (mother's sister) makes that cake.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մորեղբայրս ինձ սովորեցրեց շախմատ խաղալ։ [My uncle taught me how to play chess.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Compound word: 'մոր' (mor - mother's) + 'եղբայր' (eghbayr - brother). Literally 'mother's brother'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): MORE-EGH-BAYRs live at my *uncle's* house than you can imagine.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա սիրում է խմել մուգ սուրճ առանց շաքարի։ [She likes to drink dark coffee without sugar.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Used to describe colors or liquids (like coffee). For example, 'մուգ կապույտ' (mug kapuyt) means 'dark blue'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He drank the *dark* coffee from a MUG.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Փոքրիկ մուկը վախեցավ կատվից և թաքնվեց։ [The little mouse got scared of the cat and hid.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: մուկը (muk@). The genitive form is մկան (mkan) and the plural is մկներ (mkner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): There's a *mouse* in the MOOK-ie jar!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես պետք է մտածեմ քո առաջարկի մասին։ [I need to think about your offer.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Derived from 'միտք' (mitk' - mind, thought). Infinitive: մտածել (mtats'el). Imperative (you, singular): մտածի՛ր (mtats'ir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I *think* I'll have the MEAT AT a CELLar party.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, մտի՛ր սենյակ։ [Please, enter the room.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: մտնել (mtnel). Imperative (singular, informal): մտի՛ր (mtir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I must **enter** and find my **mittens** (mtnel).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երեկոյան ցուրտ էր, և ես մրսեցի։ [It was cold in the evening, and I caught a cold.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: մրսել (mrsel).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I caught a cold from eating too many **morsels** (mrsel) of ice cream.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Շաբաթվա մեջ կա յոթ օր։ [There are seven days in a week.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Cardinal Number.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): There are **7** people on that **yacht** (yot').

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Պապիկս այս տարի դառնում է յոթանասուն տարեկան։ [My grandpa is turning seventy years old this year.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Cardinal Number. Derived from յոթ (yot' - seven).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The **yacht** under the **sun** (yot'anasun) costs **70** million.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Տապակելու համար խնդրում եմ յուղ տուր։ [Please give me some oil/fat for frying.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: յուղը (yughy). Can mean oil, butter, or fat.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): **Yuck** (yugh), there's too much **fat** in this.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա իմ լավագույն ընկերն է։ [He/She is my best friend.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Personal Pronoun, 3rd person singular.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): **Nah** (na), **he** didn't do it.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սիրում եմ նախաճաշին ձվածեղ ուտել։ [I like to eat omelets for breakfast.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Compound word: նախ (nakha - before) + ճաշ (chash - meal). Definite form: նախաճաշը (nakhachashy).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I ate **nachos** for **breakfast** with my friend **Chaz** (nakhachash).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Կալիֆոռնիան Ամերիկայի ամենաշատ բնակչություն ունեցող նահանգն է։ [California is the most populous state in America.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: նահանգը (nahangy). Plural: նահանգներ (nahangner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Don't let the **state**'s flag just **hang** (nahang) there; raise it high!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես ընկերոջիցս երկար նամակ ստացա։ [I received a long letter from my friend.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: նամակը (namaky). Plural: նամակներ (namakner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): **No Mac** (namak) computer can send a physical **letter** like this.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նայիր, թե ինչ գեղեցիկ է արևամուտը։ [Look, how beautiful the sunset is.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: նայել (nayel). Imperative (singular, informal): նայի՛ր (nayir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I **nailed** (nayel) it! Come and **look**!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս նարինջը շատ քաղցր ու հյութալի է։ [This orange is very sweet and juicy.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: նարինջը (narinjy). Cognate with the English word 'orange' through Persian.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): This **new engine** (narinj) runs on **orange** juice.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ սենյակի պատերը նարնջագույն են։ [The walls of my room are orange.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Compound word: նարինջ (narinj - orange) + գույն (guyn - color).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The **orange-colored** **gun** (narnjaguyn) was a water pistol.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Եթե սիրում ես, պետք է կարողանաս ներել։ [If you love, you must be able to forgive.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: ներել (nerel). Imperative (singular, informal): ների՛ր (nerir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): **Near El**le, I can **forgive** anything.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ներեցե՛ք, ես ձեզ չնկատեցի։ [Sorry, I didn't notice you.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Formal/Plural imperative form of ներել (nerel - to forgive). Used to apologize.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'm **sorry** I made you **share a tea sack** (neretsek').

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ներեցեք, կարո՞ղ եք ասել՝ ինչպես հասնել հրապարակ։ [Excuse me, can you tell me how to get to the square?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Formal/Plural imperative of ներել (nerel - to forgive). Used to get attention politely.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): **Excuse me**, I have a **rare teacake** (neretsek') to show you.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Դրսում ցուրտ է, արի՛ ներս։ [It's cold outside, come inside.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb of place.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The **nurse** (ners) is waiting **inside**.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, առանց թակելու ներս մի՛ մտիր։ [Please, don't enter without knocking.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb Phrase. Combines ներս (ners - inside) and մտնել (mtnel - to enter).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The **nurse muddled** (ners mtnel) her way to **enter** the room.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Աստիճաններով զգույշ իջիր ներքև։ [Go down the stairs carefully.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb of place.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I would **never care if** (nerk'ev) I have to climb **down**.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Սա իմ ամենասիրելի նկարն է։ [This is my favorite picture.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: նկարը (nkary). Plural: նկարներ (nkarner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I took a **picture** of my **new car** (nkar).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Վան Գոգը հայտնի նկարիչ էր։ [Van Gogh was a famous artist.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. From նկար (nkar - picture) + the profession suffix -իչ (-ich). Definite form: նկարիչը (nkarichy).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The **artist**'s **new car is** (nkarich') a masterpiece.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ ննջասենյակը շատ լուսավոր է։ [My bedroom is very bright.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: ննջ- (nnj-, 'sleep') + սենյակ (senyak, 'room'). Definite form: ննջասենյակը (nnjasenyakə).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The NINJA sleeps in the SENYAK (shack) in his bedroom.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նոյեմբերին արդեն ցուրտ է լինում։ [It's already cold in November.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. An international word, recognizable in many languages.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): NO, YEM-ber, I'm not ready for the cold weather in NOVEMBER.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես նոր մեքենա եմ գնել։ [I bought a new car.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. A basic and common descriptor. Does not change for number or case.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It is NOR-mal to be excited about a NEW car.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սիրում եմ նուշ ուտել։ [I like to eat almonds.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite: նուշը (nushə). Common plural: նուշեր (nusher).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Don't give the ALMONDS a NUDGE or the bowl will fall.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս գործվածքը շատ նուրբ է։ [This fabric is very delicate.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Does not change for number or case.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The NERD handled the DELICATE butterfly with extreme care.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, նստեք։ [Please, sit down.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: նստել (nstel). Imperative: նստի՛ր (nsti՛r, singular) / նստե՛ք (nste՛k', plural).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): You have to NESTLE in to SIT comfortably on that small chair.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա գեղեցիկ դաշնամուր է նվագում։ [She plays the piano beautifully.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: նվագել (nvagel). Imperative: նվագի՛ր (nvagi՛r, singular) / նվագե՛ք (nvage՛k', plural).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): NAVIGATE the musical notes to PLAY the instrument correctly.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Սա նրա գիրքն է։ [This is his/her book.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Pronoun. Genitive (possessive) case of նա (na, 'he/she/it').
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Imagine a member of the NRA protecting HIS/HER property.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նրանց տունը շատ մեծ է։ [Their house is very big.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Pronoun. Genitive (possessive) case of նրանք (nrank', 'they').
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): NRA RANTS on the news about THEIR rights.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նրանք գնում են կինո։ [They are going to the cinema.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Pronoun. Third-person plural nominative. Root is նր- (nr-).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The NRA RANKS are huge; THEY have many members.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս շաբաթ շատ զբաղված եմ։ [This week I am very busy.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. From Hebrew 'Shabbat'. Means both 'week' and 'Saturday'. Definite: շաբաթը (shabat'ə).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I will SHOW UP AT your party on SATURDAY, at the end of the WEEK.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Շաբաթվա օրերին ես աշխատում եմ։ [On weekdays I work.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. շաբաթվա (shabat'va - 'of the week') + օրեր (orer - 'days').
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I SHOW UP FOR orders from my boss on the WEEKDAYS.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա շագանակագույն աչքեր ունի։ [She has brown eyes.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Compound word: շագանակ (shaganak, 'chestnut') + գույն (guyn, 'color'). Literally 'chestnut-color'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Imagine a SHAGGY KNOCK-A-GUY IN a BROWN trench coat.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա վերնաշապիկի տակից սպիտակ շապիկ էր հագել։ [He wore a white undershirt under his shirt.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Also used for T-shirt. Definite: շապիկը (shapikə).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A SHARP PICK from a nail tore my new UNDERSHIRT.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես քեզ շատ եմ սիրում։ [I love you very much.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb/Adjective. A highly common word used to intensify meaning.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He SHOT the basketball A LOT of times, VERY accurately.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Օգնության համար շատ շնորհակալ եմ։ [Thank you very much for the help.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. A polite, formal expression. շատ (shat, 'very') + շնորհակալ եմ (shnorhakal em, 'I am thankful').
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I SHOT NOR-CALL 'EM to say THANK YOU VERY MUCH for the gift.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Եկեք շարունակենք մեր աշխատանքը։ [Let's continue our work.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: շարունակել (sharunakel). Imperative: շարունակի՛ր (sharunaki՛r, singular) / շարունակե՛ք (sharunake՛k', plural).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): SHARON, KNOCK ELLEN out of the way so we can CONTINUE the race.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սուրճը շաքարով եմ սիրում։ [I like my coffee with sugar.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. An international word from Sanskrit 'śarkarā'. Definite form: շաքարը (shak'arə).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The baker SHOOK HER car keys to get the spilled SUGAR off them.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս շենքը շատ բարձր է։ [This building is very tall.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: շենքը (shenk'ə). Plural: շենքեր (shenk'er).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The SHANK of the nail holds the BUILDING together.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, մի շիշ ջուր տվեք։ [Please give me a bottle of water.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite: շիշը (shishə). Plural: շշեր (shsher).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The SHISH kebab marinade is in that BOTTLE.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Շնորհակալ եմ ձեր օգնության համար։ [Thank you for your help.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): A set phrase. Literally 'I am grateful'. Composed of շնորհակալ (shnorhakal - grateful) and եմ (em - I am). The root is շնորհ (shnorh), meaning 'grace' or 'favor'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'm grateful you didn't SHNORE a HACKLE of mine, thank you!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մեծ շնորհակալություն այս գեղեցիկ նվերի համար։ [Many thanks for this beautiful gift.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun form of 'thank you'. The '-ություն' (-ut'yun) suffix creates an abstract noun, similar to '-tion' or '-ness' in English. It's a more formal way to express thanks.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A 'SHNORHAKALUT'YUN' (schnorkel-salutation) is my way of saying thanks!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այսօր շատ շոգ է, արի լողալու գնանք։ [It's very hot today, let's go swimming.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: շոգ (shog). It's often used with the verb 'to be' (է - e) to describe the weather, e.g., 'շոգ է' (shog e) means 'it is hot'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It's so hot I'm in 'SHOG' (shock).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սև շոկոլադ եմ սիրում։ [I like dark chocolate.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Cognate of the international word 'chocolate'. Definite form: շոկոլադը (shokoladuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): This one is easy, it sounds just like 'chocolate'!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նոր շորեր եմ առել խանութից։ [I bought new clothes from the store.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: շոր (shor). Plural: շորեր (shorer). Definite plural: շորերը (shoreruh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It's a 'CHORE' to wash all these clothes.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մեր շունը սիրում է գնդակով խաղալ։ [Our dog loves to play with a ball.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: շուն (shun). Definite form: շունը (shunuh). Plural: շներ (shner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Don't let the dog 'SHUN' you, go play with it!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, վաղը շուտ արի։ [Please, come early tomorrow.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb. Root: շուտ (shut). Can mean 'early', 'soon', or 'fast'. 'Շուտ-շուտ' (shut-shut) means 'often' or 'quickly'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): SHUT' the door, we have to leave soon!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Զանգիր շտապ օգնություն, նա իրեն լավ չի զգում։ [Call the ambulance, he doesn't feel well.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound noun. Literally 'urgent help'. It's composed of շտապ (shtap - urgent) and օգնություն (ognut'yun - help).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): SHT'AP' (Stop)! An 'OGNUT'YUN' (ogre needs soon) help! Call the ambulance!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Պետք է շտապենք, թե չէ կուշանանք։ [We need to hurry, or else we'll be late.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: շտապել (shtapel). Imperative (singular): շտապի՛ր (shtapir). Root: շտապ (shtap).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Hurry up and 'SHTAPEL' (staple) these documents!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ո՞նց պատրաստեցիր այս համեղ տորթը։ [How did you make this delicious cake?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Interrogative adverb. An informal contraction of the more formal ինչպես (inch'pes).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): VONTS' (once) upon a time, I asked 'how'?

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Բարև, Արամ, ո՞նց ես։ [Hello, Aram, how are you?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Set phrase. A combination of Ո՞նց (vonts' - how) and ես (es - you are, 2nd person singular informal).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): VONTS' ES' (wants eggs)? Is that how you ask 'how are you'?

Example Sentence + [Translation]: — Ես չեմ գալիս։ — Ո՞նց թե չես գալիս։ [— I'm not coming. — How come you're not coming?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Interrogative phrase. Used to express surprise or disbelief, similar to 'How come?' or 'What do you mean?'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): VONTS' T'E' (wants tea)? How is that possible? I thought you wanted coffee!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ո՞վ է թակում դուռը։ Ովքե՞ր են եկել։ [Who is knocking on the door? Who (plural) has come?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Interrogative pronoun. Singular: ով (ov). Plural: ովքեր (ovk'er).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): OV' (of) course I know who did it. 'OVk'ER' (of care) are the people who came.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ո՞ր գիրքն ես ուզում կարդալ։ [Which book do you want to read?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Interrogative determiner. Root: որ (or). Can be used before a noun ('which book') or as a pronoun, often with the definite article: որը (oruh) - 'which one'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'll take this one 'OR' (or) that one? Which one?

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ողջույն, կարելի՞ է Աննային։ [Hello, may I speak to Anna?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Greeting. It comes from the word ողջ (voghj), which means 'whole', 'safe', or 'alive'. It's a slightly more formal greeting than բարև (barev).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Say hello with a 'VOGHJUYN' (voguing) pose!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: — Սուրճ կուզե՞ս։ — Ո՛չ, շնորհակալություն։ [— Do you want coffee? — No, thank you.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Negation particles. Ոչ (voch') is a standalone 'no'. Չէ (ch'e) is more versatile and often used with verbs (e.g., չեմ գալիս - I am not coming).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): VOCH' (watch) me say no. 'CH'E', that sounds like 'neh' which means no.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: — Ի՞նչ պատահեց։ — Ոչինչ, ամեն ինչ կարգին է։ [— What happened? — Nothing, everything is fine.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Pronoun. A combination of ոչ (voch' - no) and ինչ (inch' - thing/what). Literally 'no-thing'. Can also mean 'you're welcome' or 'it's ok'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): VOCH'INCH' (watch-inch) where you're going, or you'll trip on nothing.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ որդին արդեն համալսարան է գնում։ [My son is already going to university.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: որդի (ordi). Definite form: որդին (ordin). Plural: որդիներ (ordiner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My son is not a bad kid, he's just 'ORDI'-nary (ordinary).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես որոշել եմ հայերեն սովորել։ [I have decided to learn Armenian.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: որոշել (voroshel). Imperative (singular): որոշի՛ր (voroshir). Root: որոշ (vorosh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I've decided to put 'YOUR SHELL' (voroshel) on the shelf.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես ուրախ եմ, որովհետև դու այստեղ ես։ [I'm happy because you are here.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Conjunction. A compound word that connects a cause to a result.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'm late because, 'OROVHETEV' (oh, drove it heavy), the traffic was bad.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Որտե՞ղ է քո նոր գրասենյակը։ [Where is your new office?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Interrogative adverb for location (static). Root is related to 'որ' (vor - which). Contrasted with ու՞ր (ur) for direction.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): OR-TEGH' sounds like 'Or take?' me to the place. Where is it?

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սիրում եմ թեյ ու սուրճ։ [I like tea and coffee.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Conjunction. The most common way to say 'and'. Connects words or phrases of equal importance.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): U' sounds like 'You'. You and me.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ու՞մ է պատկանում այս մեքենան։ [Whom does this car belong to? / Whose car is this?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Interrogative pronoun. It is the genitive (whose) and dative/accusative (whom) case of ո՞վ (ov - who).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): UM' sounds almost identical to the English word 'Whom'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ամռանը ու՞ր եք գնալու հանգստանալու։ [Where are you going to rest in the summer?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Interrogative adverb for direction (dynamic). Often used with verbs of motion like գնալ (gnal - to go).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): UR' sounds like 'You're...'. 'You're going where?'

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես մի բաժակ ջուր եմ ուզում։ [I want a glass of water.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: ուզել (uzel). Imperative: ուզի՛ր (uzir). The present tense stem is ուզում (uzum).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): UZEL' sounds like 'Who's zealous?' 'Who's zealous enough to want that?'

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ ծննդյան տորթի վրա ութ մոմ կար։ [There were eight candles on my birthday cake.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Cardinal number. It is written 'ութ' in Armenian script.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): UT'' sounds a bit like 'eight'. Imagine someone with a Scottish accent saying 'Out!' eight times.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես երկու եղբայր ունեմ։ [I have two brothers.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: ունենալ (unenal). Imperative: ունեցի՛ր (unets'ir). This is a very common, slightly irregular verb.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): UNENAL' sounds like 'OWN IT ALL'. If you 'own it all', you have everything.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Արդեն ուշ է, պետք է քնեմ։ [It's already late, I have to sleep.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb. Can also be used as an adjective. This is the root for the verb 'to be late', ուշանալ (ushanal).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): USH' sounds like 'Whoosh!' Time went by so fast, and now I'm late.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ներողություն, որ ուշացա։ [Sorry for being late.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: ուշանալ (ushanal). Imperative: ուշացի՛ր (ushats'ir). Formed from the adverb ուշ (ush - late).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): USHANAL' sounds like 'Ooh, she null'. Her appointment is null and void because she is late.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մեր հայոց լեզվի ուսուցիչը շատ խելացի է։ [Our Armenian language teacher is very smart.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: ուսուցիչը (usuts'ich'y). Plural: ուսուցիչներ (usuts'ich'ner). Female form is ուսուցչուհի (usuts'ch'uhi).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): USUTS'ICH'' sounds like 'WHO SUITS EACH?' Who suits each student with a lesson? The teacher!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ի՞նչ ես ուզում ուտել ճաշին։ [What do you want to eat for lunch?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: ուտել (utel). Imperative is highly irregular: կե՛ր (ker).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): UTEL' sounds like 'YOU TELL'. You tell me what you want to eat.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես շատ ուրախ եմ քեզ տեսնելու համար։ [I am very happy to see you.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. The related noun is ուրախություն (urakhut'yun - happiness, joy).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): URAKH' sounds like 'YOU ROCK!'. And that makes me happy!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ուրբաթ երեկոյան ընկերներիս հետ եմ հանդիպում։ [On Friday evening I meet with my friends.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Day of the week. Unlike most other Armenian days of the week, it doesn't contain 'shab't'i'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): URBAT'' sounds like 'WHERE-BAT?' Where's the baseball bat? It's Friday, let's play!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Անձրև է գալիս, ուրեմն վերցրու քո հովանոցը։ [It's raining, so take your umbrella.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Conjunction. Used to state a conclusion or consequence. 'Therefore, so, then'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): UREMN' sounds like 'YOU'RE MEN'. You're men, so you should know better.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս խնդիրը ուրիշ լուծում ունի։ [This problem has another solution.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective or pronoun. Means 'other', 'another', or 'different'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): URISH' sounds like 'YOU WISH'. You wish you had the other one.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հիմա ժամանակ չունեմ, եկ ուրիշ անգամ խոսենք։ [I don't have time now, let's talk another time.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverbial phrase. A combination of ուրիշ (urish - other) and անգամ (angam - time/instance).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): URISH ANGAM' sounds like 'YOU WISH AGAIN'. You wish you could try again another time.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այդքան գումար ծախսելը չարժե։ [It's not worth spending that much money.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb phrase. Negative form of արժե (arzhe - it is worth). The 'չ-' (ch'-) is the negative prefix.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): CH'ARZHE' sounds like 'CHARGE?' Don't charge me for that, it's not worth it!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ով է կերել վերջին կտոր տորթը։ - Ես չեմ ասի։ [Who ate the last piece of cake? - I won't say.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb phrase. Negative future/present tense of ասել (asel - to say). First person singular: 'I won't say' or 'I am not saying'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): CH'EM ASI' sounds like 'CHEM'istry. I won't say the secret chemistry formula.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ինչ լավ եղանակ է, չէ՞ ասի։ [What nice weather, won't you say?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Interrogative phrase. A common colloquial tag question to seek agreement, like 'right?' or 'isn't it?'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): CH'E ASI' sounds like 'CHECK, a sea?' Won't you say you see the ocean?

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Չորեքշաբթի մենք հավաք ենք ունենալու։ [On Wednesday we are going to have a meeting.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Literally means 'fourth day of the week' (counting from Sunday). չորս (chors - four) + շաբթի (shabt'i - week/Saturday).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): CHOREK'SHABT'I' sounds like 'CHORE-wreck-SHABBY-tea'. I have to do chores on Wednesday, which will wreck me, so I'll just have some shabby tea.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես ունեմ չորս գիրք։ [I have four books.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: չորս (chors). Cardinal number. Does not change form.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I did my "chores" four times today.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, մի քիչ պակաս աղ լցրու։ [Please, put a little less salt.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: պակաս (pak'as). Adverb or Adjective.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): "Pack us" less stuff; the suitcase is too heavy.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ բոլոր շորերը պահարանում են։ [All my clothes are in the wardrobe.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: պահել (pahel) - to keep/store. Suffix -արան (-aran) denotes a place. Definite form: պահարանը (paharany).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A "pair" of hamsters "ran" into the wardrobe.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ պայուսակը շատ ծանր է։ [My bag is very heavy.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: պայուսակ (payusak). Definite form: պայուսակը (payusaky). Plural: պայուսակներ (payusakner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): "Pay you, sack?" No, I won't pay for this sack, I want a better bag.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Առավոտյան սիրում եմ հաց ու պանիր ուտել։ [In the morning, I like to eat bread and cheese.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: պանիր (panir). Definite form: պանիրը (paniry). Usually non-countable.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Put the cheese in a "pan near" the stove.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Պապիկս հետաքրքիր հեքիաթներ է պատմում։ [My grandfather tells interesting stories.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: պապ (pap) + diminutive suffix -իկ (-ik). Definite form: պապիկը (papiky).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My grandfather likes to "pop" his cheek; it's his little qu"irk". That's my "papik".

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Կարո՞ղ ես ինձ մի պատմություն պատմել։ [Can you tell me a story?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Infinitive: պատմել (patmel). Imperative (informal, singular): պատմի՛ր (patmir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He had to "pat me" on the el-bow to get me to tell the story.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Բացիր պատուհանը, խնդրում եմ, շատ շոգ է։ [Open the window, please, it's very hot.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: պատ (pat) - wall. Definite form: պատուհանը (patuhany). Plural: պատուհաններ (patuhanner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I put a "pot-o-honey" on the window sill.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մայրիկս համեղ ընթրիք է պատրաստում։ [My mom is preparing a delicious dinner.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Infinitive: պատրաստել (patrastel). Imperative (informal, singular): պատրաստի՛ր (patrastir). Related to the adjective պատրաստ (patrast) meaning 'ready'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): To prepare, he put the "pot roast" and steeled himself for dinner.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Սա շատ պարզ խնդիր է։ [This is a very simple problem.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: պարզ (parz). Adjective.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It's simple, just "parse" the sentence.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Յուրաքանչյուր քաղաքացի ունի իր պարտականությունները։ [Every citizen has their duties.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: պարտք (partk') - debt/obligation. The suffix -ություն (-ut'yun) creates an abstract noun. Definite form: պարտականությունը (partakanut'yuny).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It's my duty to be "part of the continuity" plan.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Եկեք այսօր երեկոյան պիցցա պատվիրենք։ [Let's order pizza tonight.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Loanword from Italian. Definite form: պիցցան (pitsan).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I want a "piece-a" pizza.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հայաստանի պրեզիդենտը այցելեց Ֆրանսիա։ [The president of Armenia visited France.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): International loanword. Definite form: պրեզիդենտը (prezidenty).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The "president" is a friendly cognate.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երեխան բարձր ջերմություն ունի։ [The child has a high fever.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: ջերմ (jerm) - warm. Suffix -ություն (-ut'yun) creates an abstract noun (warmth/fever). Definite: ջերմությունը (jermut'yuny).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): His fever was so high, it felt like "German tune"-ing fork was vibrating on his head.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մի բաժակ սառը ջուր կբերե՞ս։ [Will you bring a glass of cold water?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: ջուր (jur). Definite form: ջուրը (jury).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Are you "sure" you want that much water?

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս ռեստորանում շատ համեղ ուտեստներ կան։ [There are very delicious dishes in this restaurant.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): International loanword. Definite form: ռեստորանը (rrestorany). Plural: ռեստորաններ (rrestoranner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The "rest of Ron" is at the restaurant.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս գիրքը հարյուր ռուբլի արժե։ [This book costs one hundred rubles.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Loanword from Russian. Usually used in the plural form.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He will "rub Lee's" back for some rubles.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Սա իմ տունն է։ [This is my house.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Demonstrative pronoun for things that are close. Corresponds to the adjective այս (ays) 'this'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I "saw" this one right here.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես թեթև սալաթ եմ ուզում։ [I want a light salad.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): International loanword. Definite form: սալաթը (salat'y).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): "Salad" is an easy cognate that sounds almost the same.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ձմռանը ջուրը շատ սառն է լինում։ [In winter, the water is very cold.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: սառ (sarr). Adjective. Often becomes սառն (sarrn) before a noun starting with a vowel, e.g., սառն օդ (sarrn od) - cold air.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'm "sorry" the drink is so cold.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Սառնարանում միրգ կա՞։ [Are there fruits in the refrigerator?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: սառն (sarrn - 'cold') + -արան (-aran - suffix for 'place'). Definite form: սառնարանը (sarrnaranuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A SARAN WRAP-ARANd the refrigerator keeps the cold in.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Զգույշ, հատակին սառույց կա։ [Be careful, there is ice on the floor.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: սառ (sarr - 'cold'). Definite form: սառույցը (sarruyts'uh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'm SORRY IT'S just a block of ice.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հայաստանում շատ բարձր սարեր կան։ [There are many high mountains in Armenia.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: սարը (saruh). Plural: սարեր (sarer). The other word for mountain is լեռ (lerr).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It's SO FAR to the top of the mountain.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ընթրիքը սեղանին է։ [The dinner is on the table.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: սեղանը (seghanuh). Plural: սեղաններ (seghanner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): SAY, GONE are all the dishes from the table.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ սենյակը շատ լուսավոր է։ [My room is very bright.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: սենյակը (senyakuh). Plural: սենյակներ (senyakner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A SENNA YAK is sleeping in my room.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Սեպտեմբերին ես գնում եմ դպրոց։ [In September, I go to school.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. An international word, easy to remember. Definite form: սեպտեմբերը (septemberuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Sounds almost exactly like 'September'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սիրում եմ հայկական խոհանոցը։ [I love Armenian cuisine.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: սիրել (sirel). Imperative (singular, informal): սիրի՛ր (sirir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): SIR, I'LL tell you what I love.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս ծաղիկները շատ սիրուն են։ [These flowers are very pretty.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Derived from the word սեր (ser), meaning 'love'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): SEE a RUNning gazelle, it's so pretty.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես շատ սոված եմ, եկեք ճաշենք։ [I'm very hungry, let's have lunch.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective (participle). From the noun սով (sov), meaning 'hunger'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I feel SO VAST and empty, I must be hungry.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սովորաբար արթնանում եմ առավոտյան ժամը ութին։ [I usually wake up at eight in the morning.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb. Root: սովոր (sovor - 'habit/custom') + -աբար (-abar - adverbial suffix).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I SOAR OVER A BAR, usually, on my way to work.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա սովորում է համալսարանում։ [She is studying at the university.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: սովորել (sovorel). Imperative (singular, informal): սովորի՛ր (sovorir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): SO VORACIOUSLY I study and learn.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս անտառում շատ սունկ կա։ [There are a lot of mushrooms in this forest.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: սունկը (sunkuh). Plural: սնկեր (snker).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The mushroom SUNK the ship.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ձմռանը տաք սուպ եմ սիրում։ [I like hot soup in the winter.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. An international word. Definite form: սուպը (supuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Sounds exactly like 'soup'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Կարո՞ղ եմ մի բաժակ սուրճ խնդրել։ [Can I have a cup of coffee?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: սուրճը (surchuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I have to SEARCH for a good coffee.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նրա մեքենան սպիտակ է։ [His car is white.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Does not change for plural nouns.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Don't SPIT A CAT, it will turn white with fright.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ֆուտբոլն իմ սիրած սպորտն է։ [Football is my favorite sport.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. An international word. Definite form: սպորտը (sportuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Sounds exactly like 'sport'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա պրոֆեսիոնալ սպորտսմեն է։ [He is a professional sportsman.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. An international word. Plural: սպորտսմեններ (sports'menner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Sounds like 'sportsman'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ծննդյանս օրը շատ նվերներ ստացա։ [I got a lot of presents for my birthday.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: ստանալ (stanal). Imperative (singular, informal): ստացի՛ր (stats'ir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I STAND TALL when I get an award.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Սրանք իմ ամենասիրելի գրքերն են։ [These are my most favorite books.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Demonstrative pronoun. Plural form of սա (sa - 'this').
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): SIR, ANKLE deep are these puddles.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ամեն օր ընկերներիս հետ հանդիպում եմ սրճարանում։ [Every day I meet my friends at the cafe.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Compound word from սուրճ (surch - 'coffee') + -արան (-aran - 'place'). Definite form: սրճարանը (srcharanuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I did a SEARCH AROUND for a nice cafe.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ ընկերը սփյուռքահայ է, նա ծնվել է Ֆրանսիայում։ [My friend is a diasporan Armenian, he was born in France.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound noun: սփյուռք (sp'yurrk' - diaspora) + հայ (hay - Armenian). The plural is սփյուռքահայեր (sp'yurrk'ahayer).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He **speaks** with a **hay**-like accent because he's a diasporan Armenian.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա միշտ սև հագուստ է հագնում։ [She always wears black clothes.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: սև (sev). It does not change for plural nouns.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I **save** my black clothes for special occasions.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ պապիկը վաթսուն տարեկան է։ [My grandfather is sixty years old.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Cardinal number. Root: վեց (vets' - six), related to the '6' in '60'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): "**What soon**?" he asked, as he turned 60 and couldn't hear well.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Վաղը ես պետք է գնամ բժշկի։ [Tomorrow I have to go to the doctor.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb of time. Root: վաղ (vagh - early). The -ը ending makes it specific, 'the morrow'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'm **vague** about my plans for tomorrow.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Հանդիպումը վաղը չէ, մյուս օրն է։ [The meeting is not tomorrow, it's the day after tomorrow.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. Literally 'tomorrow is not, the other day'. Components: վաղը (tomorrow) + չէ (is not) + մյուս (other) + օրը (the day).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It's **vague** when the **che**cks will arrive, maybe the **myus** (other) day.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նրանք ուզում են իրենց հին տունը վաճառել։ [They want to sell their old house.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: վաճառել (vacharrrel). Imperative: վաճառի՛ր (vacharrrir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I have to **sell** my **watch** or **char**coal.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս խանութի վաճառողը շատ բարի է։ [The seller in this store is very kind.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun, derived from the verb վաճառել (to sell). The -ող (-ogh) suffix often indicates a person who does the action. Definite: վաճառողը (vacharrrogh@).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The **watch-a-rogue** is a seller of fake watches.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Սպասիր մեկ վայրկյան, խնդրում եմ։ [Wait one second, please.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: վայրկյանը (vayrkyan@). Plural: վայրկյաններ (vayrkyanner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A **vair**d (weird) **can**non fired in one second.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես այսօր ինձ վատ եմ զգում։ [I feel bad today.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: վատ (vat). The opposite of լավ (lav - good).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): That was a **vat** of bad soup.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նրա սիրած գույնը վարդագույնն է։ [Her favorite color is pink.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound adjective: վարդ (vard - rose) + գույն (guyn - color). Literally 'rose-color'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A pink **Varda-goon** is running through the rose garden.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Տաքսու վարորդը շատ արագ էր վարում։ [The taxi driver was driving very fast.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun, from the verb վարել (varel - to drive). Definite: վարորդը (varord@).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The driver is a **var**y **ord**inary man.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ե՞րբ կվերադառնաս տուն։ [When will you return home?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: վերադառնալ (veradarrnal). Imperative: վերադարձի՛ր (veradardzir). Prefix վերա- (vera-) means 're-' or 'again'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): **Vera, darn** it, **all** the people will return soon!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ֆիլմի վերջը շատ տխուր էր։ [The end of the movie was very sad.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: վերջը (verj@). Can also be an interjection 'Վերջ!' meaning 'Stop!' or 'Enough!'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): At the **very j**uncture of the end.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, վերցրու այս գիրքը։ [Please, take this book.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: վերցնել (verts'nel). Imperative: վերցրու՛ (verts'ru).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He will **take** the **ver**y **ts**ar's **nel**l (bell).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նայիր վերև, աստղերը շատ գեղեցիկ են։ [Look up, the stars are very beautiful.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb of place. The opposite of ներքև (nerk'ev - down).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): **Vera** is looking **up** at the roof.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես ունեմ վեց կատու։ [I have six cats.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Cardinal number. Root of վաթսուն (vats'un - sixty).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The **vets** charge six dollars for a visit.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա վիրավորված էր քո խոսքերից։ [He was insulted by your words.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Past participle, used as an adjective. From the verb վիրավորել (viravorel - to insult/wound).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I feel wounded after a **viral vor**tex of **vats**.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Դու կարող ես ինձ վստահել։ [You can trust me.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: վստահել (vst'ahel). Imperative: վստահի՛ր (vst'ahir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I **trust** you to dri**ve sta**ble and saf**el**y.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Կարո՞ղ ես ինձ մի բաժակ ջուր տալ։ [Can you give me a glass of water?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: տալ (tal). Imperative: տու՛ր (tur). Irregular verb.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He's too **tall** to **give** it to me easily.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սիրում եմ ուտել հյութալի տանձ։ [I like to eat juicy pears.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: տանձը (tandz@). Plural: տանձեր (tandzer).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He does a little **dance** for a pear.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես ունեմ տասը մատ։ [I have ten fingers.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: տաս (tas). Cardinal number. Forms the base for numbers 11-19.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I have ten fingers to 'toss' a ball.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս հեռախոսն արժե տասը հազար դրամ։ [This phone costs ten thousand drams.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound number: տասը (ten) + հազար (thousand).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It's a 'hazard' to carry 'ten thousand' dollars.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Տարին ունի տասներկու ամիս։ [The year has twelve months.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound number: տասն (ten) + երկու (two).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Toss an air-coo'kie twelve times.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ քույրը տասնինը տարեկան է։ [My sister is nineteen years old.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound number: տասն (ten) + ինը (nine).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Toss nine' pennies when you are nineteen.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ֆուտբոլային թիմում կա տասնմեկ խաղացող։ [There are eleven players in a football team.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound number: տասն (ten) + մեկ (one).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): At eleven, he 'tossed milk' all over the place.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Վաղը իմ ծննդյան օրն է, դառնում եմ տասնչորս տարեկան։ [Tomorrow is my birthday, I'm turning fourteen.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound number: տասն (ten) + չորս (four).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He 'tossed chores' aside on his fourteenth birthday.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա մեքենա վարելու իրավունք կստանա, երբ դառնա տասնվեց տարեկան։ [She will get her driver's license when she turns sixteen.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound number: տասն (ten) + վեց (six).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): For my sixteenth birthday, I want 'tossed vets'-ables (vegetables).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ տատիկը շատ համեղ տոլմա է պատրաստում։ [My grandmother makes very delicious dolma.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: տատ (tat). Definite form: տատիկը (tatik@). A diminutive, affectionate form of տատ (tat).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My grandmother gives me a gentle pat-pat, she is my 'tatik'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մենք ունենք տարբեր կարծիքներ, բայց մենք ընկերներ ենք։ [We have different opinions, but we are friends.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: տարբեր (tarber). Adjective. Does not decline for number or case when used before a noun.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The bears at the 'tar pit are different'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մեկ տարին ունի տասներկու ամիս։ [One year has twelve months.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: տար (tar). Definite form: տարին (tarin). Plural: տարիներ (tariner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Driving my car for a 'year' put a lot of 'tar' on 'it'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Զգույշ, սուրճը շատ տաք է։ [Be careful, the coffee is very hot.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: տաք (tak'). Adjective.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Don't step on that 'hot tack'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Կարո՞ղ ենք տաքսիով գնալ օդանավակայան։ [Can we go to the airport by taxi?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. International loanword. Definite form: տաքսին (tak'sin).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It's a 'taxi', sounds almost the same!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես պետք է հիմա տաքսի պատվիրեմ։ [I need to order a taxi now.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb phrase. Infinitive: պատվիրել (patvirel). Imperative: պատվիրի՛ր (patvirir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): To 'order a taxi', I'll ask the driver to 'pat a viral' star.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մի՛ անհանգստացիր, տաքսին կգա հինգ րոպեից։ [Don't worry, the taxi will come in five minutes.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Phrase. կգա (kga) is the future tense of գալ (gal - to come). րոպեից (ropeyits') is the ablative case, meaning 'from (a point in) time'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The 'taxi will come in 5 minutes', so we 'k'ga' (can go) soon.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մեր ընտանիքը տեղափոխվել է նոր բնակարան։ [Our family has moved to a new apartment.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: տեղափոխվել (teghap'okhvel). Imperative: տեղափոխվի՛ր (teghap'okhvir). From տեղ (tegh - place) and փոխել (p'okhel - to change).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): To 'move place', I had to 'take a fox well' with me.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես ուզում եմ տեսնել Արարատ լեռը։ [I want to see Mount Ararat.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: տեսնել (tesnel). Imperative: տե՛ս (tes).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I can 'see' you're having a 'tense nail'-biting moment.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Գրի՛ր այս բառերը քո տետրի մեջ։ [Write these words in your notebook.]
