Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: տետր (tetr). Definite form: տետրը (tetr@). Plural: տետրեր (tetrer).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I used to play 'Tetris' in my school 'notebook'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա տխուր էր, որովհետև նրա ընկերը գնացել էր։ [He was sad because his friend had left.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: տխուր (tkhur).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'm so 'sad', I could just say 't'curse' this day!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այգում մի տղա ֆուտբոլ է խաղում։ [A boy is playing football in the park.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: տղան (tghan). Plural: տղաներ (tghaner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): That young 'boy' is wearing a Roman 'toga'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Դպրոցի տնօրենը խոսում էր աշակերտների հետ։ [The school director was talking to the students.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. From տուն (tun - house) + օրենք (orenk' - law/rule), literally 'ruler of the house'. Definite form: տնօրենը (tnoren@).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The 'director' had a 'ton of rain' fall on his parade.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այսօր ծնունդս եմ տոնելու ընկերներիս հետ։ [Today I'm going to celebrate my birthday with my friends.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: տոնել (tonel). Imperative: տոնի՛ր (tonir). From the root word տոն (ton), which means 'holiday' or 'festival'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Let's set the TONE ALL happy to celebrate!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երեկոյան ես միշտ շտապում եմ տուն։ [In the evening, I always hurry home.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: տուն (tun). Definite form: տունը (tun@). The locative case 'at home' is տանը (tan@).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My home is playing a catchy TUNE.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, սպասիր ընդամենը հինգ րոպե։ [Please, wait just five minutes.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: րոպե (rope). Plural: րոպեներ (ropener).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I can climb the ROPE in one minute.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երեխաները ուրախ-ուրախ ցատկում են։ [The children are jumping happily.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: ցատկել (ts'atkel). Imperative: ցատկի՛ր (ts'atkir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The CAT KILLED it when it had to jump.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Անձրևից հետո ամեն տեղ ցեխ էր։ [After the rain, there was mud everywhere.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: ցեխ (ts'ekh). Definite form: ցեխը (ts'ekh@).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My expensive CHECK shirt is now covered in mud.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ցերեկը ես աշխատանքի եմ գնում։ [During the daytime, I go to work.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun/Adverb. Root: ցերեկ (ts'erek). Often contrasted with գիշեր (gisher) - 'night'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I can only see the SEA WRECK during the daytime.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ամեն առավոտ ես ցնցուղ եմ ընդունում։ [Every morning I take a shower.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb Phrase. The noun is ցնցուղ (ts'nts'ugh), 'shower'. The verb is ընդունել (@ndunel), 'to accept/take'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I CHIN-CHUG a lot of water when I take a shower.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ձմռանը Հայաստանում շատ ցուրտ է լինում։ [In winter, it is very cold in Armenia.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: ցուրտ (ts'urt).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It's so cold, it makes your tooth HURT.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ցտեսություն, կհանդիպենք վաղը։ [Goodbye, we'll see each other tomorrow.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Interjection. A formal goodbye, literally means 'until seeing'. A combination of ց- (ts'-) 'until' + տես- (tes-) 'see'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It's nice to SEE YOU SOON. Goodbye!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Խնդրում եմ, ստուգեք, արդյոք դուռը փակ է։ [Please, check if the door is closed.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: փակ (p'ak). It is the past participle of the verb փակել (p'akel), 'to close'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): You have to PACK your bags before the door is closed for boarding.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մի՛ մոռացիր փակել լուսամուտը։ [Don't forget to close the window.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: փակել (p'akel). Imperative: փակի՛ր (p'akir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): PACK ALL your things and close the door.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս բարձը շատ փափուկ է։ [This pillow is very soft.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. Root: փափուկ (p'ap'uk).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My PUPPY is so soft.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Փետրվարը տարվա ամենակարճ ամիսն է։ [February is the shortest month of the year.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: փետրվար (p'et'rvar). One of the 12 months.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): In February, I'll PET a VULTURE.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Կենդանաբանական այգում մեծ փիղ տեսանք։ [We saw a big elephant at the zoo.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: փիղ (p'igh). Plural: փղեր (p'gher).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I'll give a FIG to the elephant.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես իմ բանալիներն եմ փնտրում։ [I am searching for my keys.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: փնտրել (p'ntrel). Imperative: փնտրի՛ր (p'ntrir).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I search for a good printer until I can PRINT REAL copies.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես բավականաչափ փող չունեմ այս գիրքը գնելու համար։ [I don't have enough money to buy this book.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: փող (p'ogh). The word for 'change' (coins) is մանր (manr).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It costs a lot of money to buy a POG collection.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մեր փողոցը շատ հանգիստ է։ [Our street is very quiet.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: փողոց (p'oghots'). Definite form: փողոցը (p'oghots'@).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A POG GOAT is running down the street.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ստուգիր էլեկտրոնային փոստդ, ես քեզ նամակ եմ ուղարկել։ [Check your email, I sent you a letter.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: փոստ (p'ost). From Russian/International 'post'. Often used as էլ․ փոստ (el. p'ost) for 'e-mail'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I tied the mail to the fence POST.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես ուզում եմ փորձել այդ նոր ռեստորանը։ [I want to try that new restaurant.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Verb. Infinitive: փորձել (p'ordzel). Imperative: փորձի՛ր (p'ordzir). The noun փորձ (p'ordz) means 'experience' or 'attempt'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Try a pretzel from PORT ZELDA.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Աշնանը ես տերևները հավաքում եմ փոցխով։ [In the autumn, I collect the leaves with a rake.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: փոցխ (p'ots'kh). An instrument used for gardening.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The FOX stole my rake!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես ապրում եմ փոքր տանը։ [I live in a small house.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: փոքր (p'ok'r). An adjective that typically precedes the noun it modifies.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The POKER chips I won were very SMALL.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երևանը մեծ և գեղեցիկ քաղաք է։ [Yerevan is a big and beautiful city.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: քաղաք (k'aghak'). Definite form: քաղաքը (k'aghak'e). Plural: քաղաքներ (k'aghak'ner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): He drove a CAR that was LACKing a roof through the CITY.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես Հայաստանի քաղաքացի եմ։ [I am a citizen of Armenia.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: քաղաքացի (k'aghak'ats'i). Derived from քաղաք (k'aghak', 'city') + ացի (-ats'i, 'inhabitant of'). Definite form: քաղաքացին (k'aghak'ats'in).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The CITY's CITIZEN has to SIT down.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս տորթը շատ քաղցր է։ [This cake is very sweet.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: քաղցր (k'aghts'r). A simple adjective describing taste.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The candy was so SWEET it felt like it CUTS your throat.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սիրում եմ քայլել այգում։ [I like to walk in the park.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Infinitive: քայլել (k'aylel). Imperative (you, singular): քայլի՛ր (k'ayli'r).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I like to WALK with KYLIE.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Քանի՞ տարեկան ես։ [How old are you? (lit: How many years old are you?)]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Interrogative pronoun/adverb. Used to ask about quantity.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): HOW MANY CANS of soda do you have?

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ հայրը քառասուն տարեկան է։ [My father is 40 years old.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Cardinal number. From քառ- (k'arr-, 'four') + -սուն (-sun, suffix for tens). Literally 'four-tens'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): A CAR AS SOON as it turns 40 needs a check-up.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ քեռին բժիշկ է։ [My uncle (mother's brother) is a doctor.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Specifically refers to the maternal uncle (mother's brother), distinct from the paternal uncle (հորեղբայր). Definite form: քեռին (k'errin).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My UNCLE (mother's side) loves KERRYgold butter.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես քիչ հայերեն եմ խոսում։ [I speak a little Armenian.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adverb/Adjective. Means 'a little' or 'few'. Often used with մի (mi) as մի քիչ (mi k'ich') for 'a little bit'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I only want a LITTLE bit of QUICHE.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես ուզում եմ շուտ քնել այսօր։ [I want to sleep early today.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Infinitive: քնել (k'nel). Imperative (you, singular): քնի՛ր (k'ni'r).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): You should KNEEL by the bed before you SLEEP.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Սա քո՞ գիրքն է։ [Is this your book?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Possessive pronoun, 2nd person singular informal. It always precedes the noun.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Is that YOUR COW?

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ քույրը ինձնից մեծ է։ [My sister is older than me.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Definite form: քույրը (k'uyre). Plural: քույրեր (k'uyrer).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My SISTER is way COOLER than I am.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս գիրքը քսան դոլար արժե։ [This book costs 20 dollars.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Cardinal number for twenty.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Can you buy 20 cans of tuna?

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Կարո՞ղ ես ինձ օգնել։ [Can you help me?]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Infinitive: օգնել (ognel). Imperative (you, singular): օգնի՛ր (ogni'r). The person being helped is in the dative case (e.g., ինձ, 'to me').
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The OAK BELL is rung when you need HELP.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Օգնությո՛ւն, ես մոլորվել եմ։ [Help, I am lost!]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Formed from the verb օգնել (ognel) + the suffix -ություն (-ut'yun) to create an abstract noun.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The OAK NEW TUNE is the signal for HELP.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Օգոստոսին Հայաստանում շատ շոգ է։ [In August, it is very hot in Armenia.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun, month of the year. Loanword from Latin 'Augustus'. Locative case (in August) is Օգոստոսին (Ogostosin).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): In AUGUST, OH, GO TOAST us some marshmallows.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այս բառարանը շատ օգտակար է։ [This dictionary is very useful.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Adjective. A compound word from օգուտ (ogut, 'benefit') and -ակար (-akar, 'having'), meaning 'benefit-having' or 'beneficial'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): An OAK CAR is very USEFUL for carrying heavy things.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Լեռներում օդը մաքուր է։ [The air in the mountains is clean.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Root: օդ (od). A simple noun. Definite form: օդը (ode).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It's ODD that the AIR is purple here.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ինքնաթիռը վայրէջք կատարեց «Զվարթնոց» օդանավակայանում։ [The plane landed at 'Zvartnots' airport.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Compound noun: օդ (od, 'air') + նավ (nav, 'ship') + կայան (kayan, 'station'). Literally an 'air-ship-station'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): At the AIRPORT, it was ODD a NAVY ship had a CANYON on it.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Երևանի Օպերայի շենքը շատ գեղեցիկ է։ [The Yerevan Opera building is very beautiful.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. An international loanword. Definite form: օպերան (operan).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Let's go to the OPERA.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այսօր շատ արևոտ օր է։ [Today is a very sunny day.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: օր (or). Definite form: օրը (oruh). Plural: օրեր (orer).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): OR it could be night, but right now it's a new day.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես աշխատում եմ մեծ օֆիսում։ [I work in a big office.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: օֆիս (ofis). A loanword from Russian/International 'office'. Definite form: օֆիսը (ofisuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): OH, FEAST! They brought donuts to the office!

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Այդ ֆաբրիկան կահույք է արտադրում։ [That factory produces furniture.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: ֆաբրիկա (fabrika). A loanword from Russian 'фабрика'. Definite form: ֆաբրիկան (fabrikan).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): They FABRICATE everything in that factory.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Իմ պապիկը ֆերմա ունի գյուղում։ [My grandfather has a farm in the village.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: ֆերմա (ferma). A loanword from Russian/International 'farm'. Definite form: ֆերման (ferman).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): The ground on this farm is very FIRM-A.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ֆիզիկան բարդ, բայց հետաքրքիր գիտություն է։ [Physics is a difficult but interesting science.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: ֆիզիկա (fizika). International word. Definite form: ֆիզիկան (fizikan).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): It was quite a FEAT-SEEKER to pass my physics exam.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Եկեք երեկոյան նոր ֆիլմ դիտենք։ [Let's watch a new film in the evening.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: ֆիլմ (film). International word. Definite form: ֆիլմը (filmuh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): This Armenian film sounds just like the English word 'film'.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Նա ֆինանսների ոլորտի մասնագետ է։ [She is a specialist in the field of finance.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: ֆինանս (finans). International word. Often used in plural: ֆինանսներ (finansner).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): To get good at finance, you need to FIND NANCE.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Սա իմ ամենասիրելի ընտանեկան ֆոտոն է։ [This is my most favorite family photo.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: ֆոտո (foto). International word, short for 'photograph'. Definite form: ֆոտոն (foton).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): I've got-A FOTO of you! It's a photo.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Տղաները բակում ֆուտբոլ են խաղում։ [The boys are playing football in the yard.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Noun. Root: ֆուտբոլ (futbol). International word. Definite form: ֆուտբոլը (futboluh).
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): My FOOT is on the BALL, I'm playing football.

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Ես սիրում եմ խնձոր և տանձ։ [I like apples and pears.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Conjunction. Root: և (ev). This is the formal or written form of 'and'.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Think of Adam and EVE (ev).

Example Sentence + [Translation]: Մայրիկն ու հայրիկը տանն են։ [Mom and Dad are at home.]
Grammar (Root/Infinitive + Imperative): Conjunctions. և (ev) is formal/written, connecting clauses or in formal speech. ու (u) is informal/spoken, typically used between two words.
Cheat Phrase (Mnemonic): Me and YOU (u) are friends.
